1	# Epic 3 Retrospective — Consolidated OIDC Release Workflow
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-04-21
4	**Facilitator:** bmad-retrospective (Amelia)
5	**Project Lead:** Armel
6	**Epic status at retro:** all 3 stories `done`; epic already flipped to `done` at Story 3.3 close-out; retrospective key `epic-3-retrospective` flipped `optional` → `done` as part of this retro.
7	**Previous retrospective:** Epic 2 (2026-04-21).
8	
9	## Summary
10	
11	Epic 3 consolidated `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` + local `release:*` scripts into a single canonical `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (254 lines on `main`) using OIDC Trusted Publishing with auto-provenance (SLSA Build Level 2), reviewer-gated `release` Environment, concurrency protection, dist-tag routing, CHANGELOG append with preamble-restore, and full pre-publish validation. Proven live: `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` published to npm with verified provenance attestation via Story 3.2's alpha cut. Legacy workflows retained in-repo as inert backup — `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger neutralized, both legacy files carry `DEPRECATED` headers. All three stories landed on **2026-04-21**.
12	
13	Delivered via two PRs to `main`:
14	
15	- **PR #193** merge-commit `14cedfe` — consolidated feature branch (23 commits) including Story 3.1 (commit `1295bf6` + 5 review patches → `34ad130`), Story 3.2 (4 load-bearing defect-fixes on `release.yaml` + 1 superseded-but-preserved + 1 net-zero temp-trigger pair), Story 3.2 review patch (`535e38f` npm floor assertion), Story 3.3 Patches A/B/C (`ab74c10`, `76a62cd`, `10c1134`).
16	- **PR #194** merge-commit `b662bce` — Story 3.3 code-review hardening patches (`6a6a521`) on Patch C's awk preamble-restore step.
17	
18	| Story | Outcome | Review result |
19	|-------|---------|---------------|
20	| 3.1 Author canonical `release.yaml` (`1295bf6` + `34ad130`) | 174-line workflow greenfield-created on `feat/release-workflow`; `[Unreleased]` anchor pre-inserted; Windows-cell `conventional-changelog-cli` smoke added to `quality.yaml`; file NOT merged (Story 3.3 territory) | 3 decision-needed + 3 patch + 5 defer; Acceptance Auditor all-green on ACs #1–#18 |
21	| 3.2 Validate `release.yaml` via alpha cut (4 defect-fix commits + `535e38f`) | Run `24714953668` succeeded on 5th attempt; `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` published under `--tag alpha` with `attestations.provenance.predicateType = https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1`; `dist-tags.latest` unchanged at `0.10.0`; GitHub Release `v0.10.1-alpha.0` created with `prerelease: true`; allow-list widen/revoke cycle clean; **HIGH finding: CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle structurally broken** by preset's byte-0 prepend | 1 HIGH (deferred to 3.3) + 2 MED + 3 LOW + 2 INFO dev-surfaced + 1 patch (`535e38f`) + 6 defer from formal review |
22	| 3.3 Merge `release.yaml` to `main` (`ab74c10` + `76a62cd` + `10c1134` + `6a6a521`) | Patch A (neutralize `publish.yaml` `v*` trigger) + Patch B (DEPRECATED headers on both legacy files) + Patch C (CHANGELOG preamble-restore awk step) landed atomically in PR #193; post-merge code review found 4 Patch C hardening gaps (empty-file guard, anchored regex, `seen_title` assertion, post-awk empty-output check) fixed in PR #194 | 4 code-review patches (1 HIGH + 3 MED) all applied; AC drift flagged on AC #5 (stale check names) and AC #6 (GitHub self-approval semantics) |
23	
24	Commits on `feat/release-workflow` for Epic 3: ~13 spanning Stories 3.1–3.3 plus the code-review hardening commit on the follow-up PR. Feature branch preserved post-merge at `6a6a521` (NFR12 reversibility boundary — alpha tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` points at orphaned CI-ephemeral commit `2a57dcbd` reachable only via the tag + branch ancestry chain).
25	
26	## What went well
27	
28	1. **Epic 2 retro carry-forwards landed 6-for-6.** (A) tooling — stayed on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` with rationale captured in commit body; (B) `[Unreleased]` anchor pre-inserted at Story 3.1 Task 4; (C) bogus-issue-ref bash guard added and later widened to `(closes|fixes|resolves)` in Story 3.1 code review; (D) `.yaml` vs `.yml` triple-guard held — and a bonus fourth guard surfaced: ESLint `yml/file-extension` rule (`eslint.config.mjs:65-72`) enforces `.yaml` at lint time; (E) Windows-cell smoke step added to `quality.yaml`'s `validate` job; Story 3.3 precondition — atomic neutralization of `publish.yaml` `v*` tag trigger — bundled into PR #193 as Patch A. Nothing orphaned from Epic 2 retro.
29	2. **Local dry-run caught an AC-literal bug at authoring-time — new "dry-run gate" pattern validated.** Story 3.3 AC #3.C.i's verbatim awk would have produced `# Changelog → preamble → new release → [Unreleased] → older` (inverting the anchor/new-release pair vs. the Keep-a-Changelog shape the *same* AC stated in prose). Dev-agent Task 5 dry-run on `git show v0.10.1-alpha.0:CHANGELOG.md` as test fixture found the bug; one-line fix (removed the erroneous `section==1 && /^## \[Unreleased\]/ { section=2 }` rule); stayed in approach (C.i), no escalation to Python helper needed. Lesson for future stories: **ACs that contain inline scripts MUST be dry-run at story-authoring time, not only at dev-agent time.**
30	3. **OIDC chain worked end-to-end against real infrastructure.** Run `24714953668` — `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (no token fallback), no `--provenance` flag (auto under OIDC), `.yaml` filename matched TP registration. `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0 --json` showed `attestations.provenance.predicateType = https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1`; `npm audit signatures` on a fresh install reported verified attestation. NFR4 (100% provenance) operationally satisfied for the first time.
31	4. **Audit-trail discipline held under 4 iterations of defect-fixes.** Story 3.2 burned 4 failed alpha-cut runs before succeeding. Zero amends, zero force-pushes — even the superseded `f449c67` (first `--force` attempt that didn't work, replaced by `cbc7851` side-prefix install) kept in history. The commit ledger (`e470501 → f449c67 → cbc7851 → de2334b → cae5861 → 24714953668 green → 7208ff4 revert`) reads as a linear narrative of the debugging path. This is AC #11 "no amend, no force-push" being load-bearing for post-incident forensics.
32	5. **Forward-flagging + post-merge hardening discipline.** Story 3.1 review patched Story 3.3's scope (Patches A/B/C bundled from three separate deferred-work entries spanning Epic 1 + Epic 2 + Story 3.1 + Story 3.2). Story 3.2's HIGH `[Unreleased]`-lifecycle finding flowed straight into Story 3.3 Patch C at create-story phase. After Story 3.3 merge, `bmad-code-review` on fresh context surfaced four edge-case vulnerabilities in Patch C's awk (empty CHANGELOG.md, prefix-matching title header, embedded `# Changelog ...` in commit bodies, awk exit-zero-with-empty-output) — all fixed in PR #194 (`6a6a521`) before any future release could hit them.
33	6. **Scope envelope held across all three stories.** Each story's file-list AC enumerated forbidden files; every commit diff matched expectation. No drive-by `release:*` script edits, no `package.json` drive-bys, no `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook` prose pre-emption (Story 4.1 territory), no `CONTRIBUTING.md` edits (Story 4.2 territory).
34	
35	## Patterns across all three stories
36	
37	### Preset-behavior assumptions cannot be validated by hand-reading — only by running the preset
38	
39	Story 3.1 pre-inserted `## [Unreleased]` expecting `conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` to honor the anchor as the insertion point. It doesn't — the preset prepends at byte-0 of the infile unconditionally, pushing `# Changelog` title + preamble + `[Unreleased]` anchor DOWN below the new entry. Only Story 3.2's concrete alpha cut exposed this; the file-ordering damage compounds with every release cut. Story 3.3 Patch C (awk preamble-restore) remediated it. **Generalization:** when a story design rests on assumed third-party tool behavior, the alpha/smoke cut IS the validator — accept that upfront, plan the fix into the downstream story, don't wait for post-hoc surprise.
40	
41	### Asymmetric error-handling in husky hooks is a CI-gotcha class-of-bug
42	
43	Story 3.2's `Commit version bump` failure traced to `.husky/commit-msg` using strict `|| exit 1` when invoking the local-only `entire` command, while sibling hooks (`post-commit`, `prepare-commit-msg`, `pre-push`) gracefully degrade via `|| true`. Only `commit-msg` hard-blocks CI. Fix: `HUSKY: "0"` at job level (husky's documented opt-out). Defense-in-depth recommendation tracked in deferred-work: belt-and-braces add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call so a future matrix split or reusable-workflow extraction that drops the env doesn't silently reintroduce the failure.
44	
45	### Dev-agent commit-body `AC #N` → preset parses `#N` as GH issue ref
46	
47	Four Story 3.2 defect-fix commit bodies referenced `AC #11` / `AC #12` meaning Acceptance Criteria. The `conventionalcommits` preset interpreted these as GitHub issue references and emitted `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` links in the CHANGELOG for `0.10.1-alpha.0`. The bogus-issue-ref guard correctly did NOT fire (refs are numeric; guard targets the Story 2.2 class-of-bug of non-numeric refs). Cosmetic pollution in the immutable alpha CHANGELOG entry, not blocking. **Process fix for all future dev agents:** never write `AC #N` in commit bodies — write `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N`. `#N` is reserved for genuine GitHub issue refs.
48	
49	### AC drift surfaced at Story 3.3 execution — Task 1 recon should probe for it
50	
51	Story 3.3 AC #5 enumerated seven `quality.yaml` required status check names (`quality.yaml / schemas|refs|tests`); actual ruleset `13855503` names are `prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)`. Same coverage shape, different job IDs — story text drifted. Story 3.3 AC #6 assumed `prevent_self_review: false` on the `release` environment permits self-approval on one's own PR. **Wrong semantic:** GitHub's API rejects self-approval on one's own PR *unconditionally*, regardless of env-level `prevent_self_review`. The solo-maintainer path is admin-bypass via the ruleset's bypass-list (`RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request`), which ruleset `13855503` was already designed with — but the story text didn't know. **Generalization:** hard-coded check names, ruleset/env IDs, and API-semantic assumptions must be verified against live state at Task 1 recon — not trusted from the AC.
52	
53	### `--force` doesn't unblock an in-flight arborist rebuild race
54	
55	Story 3.2 `f449c67` tried `npm install -g npm@latest --force` to dodge a self-upgrade race on the GitHub-hosted runner's bundled npm. The `--force` flag didn't help because the race is about `rebuild.js` reading `promise-retry` from a now-partial `lib/node_modules/npm/` tree mid-install. `cbc7851` superseded with a side-prefix install (`$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp` + `$GITHUB_PATH` prepend) — the old npm's tree stays intact, subsequent steps resolve to the new npm. Plus `535e38f` (added post-review) now asserts the `$GITHUB_PATH` propagation actually worked by running bare `npm --version` and comparing against a `sort -V`-based `11.5.1` floor. Root-cause understanding matters — the shortcut flag didn't address the actual race.
56	
57	## Load-bearing inputs carried into Epic 4 and beyond
58	
59	Epic 4 is planned doc-only per `epics.md` (Story 4.1 rollback playbook + Story 4.2 CONTRIBUTING.md deprecation prose + `release:*` script fail-loud prefixes). The following items are NOT folded into Epic 4 — they're queued for Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit or post-v1.0.0 hardening per the current epic sequencing. `deferred-work.md` is the source of truth; each item below has a named home.
60	
61	### Release-workflow hardening (Story 5.1 audit or post-v1.0.0)
62	
63	**(F1) Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` classification + empty-`$VERSION` → `@latest` fallback.** `release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish) — both chains. Future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain; empty `$VERSION` routes to `@latest` silently. Fix: semver-aware parsing or anchoring to prerelease identifier segment + `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]] && exit 1` guard on both chains. Tracked from Story 3.2 code review; doubled via dry-run mirror in commit `de2334b`.
64	
65	**(F2) Tag-push-before-npm-publish ordering.** `release.yaml:161-181`. OIDC failure after tag-push orphans `vX.Y.Z` on `origin` without an npm artifact. Story 4.1 Scenario E (manual recovery) documents the prose fix; structural fix (tag-after-publish reorder or compensating tag-delete on publish failure) belongs with a dedicated hardening story post-v1.0.0.
66	
67	**(F3) Zero-conventional-commit release path.** `release.yaml:119` Update CHANGELOG step — preset prepends nothing, Patch C's preamble-restore correctly no-ops, but workflow still proceeds to tag + publish with no CHANGELOG entry. Candidate for a post-v1.0.0 release-safety hardening pass — detect empty prepend and `exit 1`.
68	
69	**(F4) Husky defense-in-depth.** `HUSKY: "0"` at job level is the sole safety net. A matrix split or reusable-workflow extraction could drop the env and silently reintroduce Story 3.2's `commit-msg` failure. Belt-and-braces: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` invocation in the `Commit version bump` step.
70	
71	**(F5) Non-conventional commit prefix `release:`.** `release.yaml:125`. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat|fix|chore|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|revert`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless today under `HUSKY=0`, doubles the failure surface if that env is ever dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):` — bundle with (F4) defense-in-depth.
72	
73	**(F6) Feature-branch alpha-cut tags orphan on branch deletion.** Tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` points at ephemeral commit `2a57dcbd` reachable only via the tag + `feat/release-workflow` ancestry. Post-merge branch deletion (common habit from other PR workflows) + GitHub retention GC could break `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` in future runs. Story 3.3 AC #10 enforces branch preservation; longer-term structural fix (cherry-pick alpha-cut commits to `main` post-validation, or change workflow to require `main` dispatch) belongs with Story 4.1 rollback semantics or a dedicated hardening story.
74	
75	### Post-v1.0.0 supply-chain hardening (dedicated story or Story 5.1)
76	
77	**(G1) SHA-pin third-party actions.** `release.yaml` uses `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4` — floating major-version tags. With `id-token: write` + `contents: write`, a compromised maintainer or compromised tag could exfiltrate. Industry best practice. Consistent with repo-wide patterns elsewhere, so not a regression — but worth a dedicated supply-chain pass post-v1.0.0.
78	
79	**(G2) `npm install -g npm@latest` unpinned.** `release.yaml:52`. Future `npm@12.x` with breaking OIDC-surface changes would silently break the next release. `535e38f` now asserts floor `≥11.5.1` post-install, but does not prevent a major-version break. Patch-grade fix: `npm@^11.5.1`.
80	
81	**(G3) Node 20 deprecation warnings.** Repo-wide — fires on every `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`. GitHub forcing Node 24 by 2026-06-02; Node 20 removed 2026-09-16. Two paths: rely on action maintainers to publish Node-24 versions (most have), or set `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` at workflow level now. Post-v1.0.0 maintenance story.
82	
83	**(G4) `uv` cache-miss warning on every release run.** `release.yaml:54-57`. `setup-uv` looks for `requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml` / `uv.lock`; none present so cache never fills. Low-priority cleanup — add a Python `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock` or silence via `cache-dependency-glob`.
84	
85	### Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit backlog (inherited + new from Epic 3)
86	
87	**(H1)** `commands/install.js:23` silent `exit 0` on `{success: false}` — contract gap with STABILITY.md's "`skf install` SHALL exit 1 on unhandled failure" (Epic 2 carry-over, still unresolved).
88	
89	**(H2)** `package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint (Epic 2 carry-over, still unresolved).
90	
91	**(H3)** `marketplace.json` unanchored sed fragility if schema grows a second `"version"` field (Story 3.1 known-fragility, acknowledged in spec).
92	
93	**(H4)** Starlight sidebar entry missing for `docs/STABILITY.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` (Epic 1 + Epic 2 carry-over).
94	
95	**(H5)** Hand-curate v1.0.0 CHANGELOG from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` workshop file (Epic 2 carry-over; Story 5.1 owns the final prose).
96	
97	**(H6)** DRAFT → STABLE banner flip on `docs/STABILITY.md` (Epic 2 carry-over; Story 5.1 owns).
98	
99	**(H7)** `0.10.1-alpha.0` CHANGELOG's `closes [#11]` / `[#12]` cosmetic refs from dev-agent commit-body `AC #N` pollution — decide: hand-correct during Story 5.1 audit, or accept as historical artifact (alpha is explicit throwaway per Story 3.2 AC #9).
100	
101	**(H8)** One-time hand-bump path `0.10.0 → 1.0.0-rc.0` before `version_bump: rc` for Story 5.2. `npm version prerelease --preid=rc` on `0.10.0` produces `0.10.1-rc.0`, not `1.0.0-rc.0`. Procedure: `npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version` on a pre-cut commit to `main` via PR, then dispatch `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc` to advance to `1.0.0-rc.1`. Story 5.1 documents this in `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`.
102	
103	**(H9)** `prevent_self_review: false → true` flip on the `release` environment when a second reviewer is onboarded. Epic 1 standing flag; now clarified per Story 3.3 AC #6 drift — this is about *ruleset bypass-list revoke* (remove `RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request` bypass) in addition to the env-level flag flip. Full solo-maintainer-workflow-to-multi-reviewer transition needs both.
104	
105	### CI-as-enforced-gate candidates (standalone story post-v1.0.0 or Story 5.1)
106	
107	Carry-over from Epic 1 + Epic 2 retros — prose-only constraints that CI could enforce:
108	
109	**(I1)** `lychee CHANGELOG.md` or `markdown-link-check` CI step scoped to the changelog — regression guard against Story 2.2's `closes [#F59E0B]` class-of-bug.
110	
111	**(I2)** `release` env zero-secrets assertion — `gh api /environments/release/secrets` must return `total_count: 0`, fail CI otherwise.
112	
113	**(I3)** Name-based lookup instead of hard-coded ruleset `13855503` / environment `14347249917` IDs in `docs/RELEASING.md`.
114	
115	**(I4)** Trusted Publisher registration shape vs repo reality assertion — CI reads `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` table and verifies `.github/workflows/release.yaml` exists, environment `release` exists, `package.json` name matches.
116	
117	**(I5)** `package.json.devDependencies` mirror-entry drift guard in `package-lock.json.packages[""].devDependencies` — lightweight hygiene (Epic 2 carry-over).
118	
119	### No Story 4.2 / Story 3.3 overlap cleanup needed
120	
121	Story 3.3 Patch B shipped the DEPRECATED comment header wording on both `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` verbatim matching Story 4.2's AC reference text. Story 4.2 scope narrows to CONTRIBUTING.md "Releasing" section prose + `release:*` script fail-loud prefixes in `package.json`. No re-edit of workflow headers needed.
122	
123	## Commitments
124	
125	### Carry-forward into Epic 4
126	
127	1. **Story 4.1 `create-story` MUST import `deferred-work.md § Story 3.1/3.2/3.3 code review` entries for Scenarios D/E/F.** Scenario E (npm publish succeeded but tag/push failed) has a concrete answer from Story 3.2's observed ordering (tag pushed before publish — the risk is inverted from the AC's premise). Scenario D (tag exists but publish failed) maps directly to Story 3.2's orphaned-commit pattern. Scenario F (OIDC compromise) references Story 1.3's TP registration surface.
128	2. **Story 4.1 MUST add a "release.yaml disabled / reverted" scenario** per Story 3.3 code-review defer. Low probability, high-consequence fallback gap when `release.yaml` is the single release root.
129	3. **Story 4.2 `create-story` MUST NOT re-edit the legacy workflow headers** — Story 3.3 Patch B already landed them verbatim. Scope narrows to CONTRIBUTING.md + `package.json release:*` prefixes.
130	4. **Story 4.1 MUST use name-based lookups** (`gh api .../rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id'`) NOT hard-coded ruleset/env IDs in any new prose — Epic 1 code-review defer carries over.
131	
132	### Process improvements (continue across Epic 4+)
133	
134	5. **Live `gh api` / UI recon block at Task 1 of every story.** 9-for-9 across Epics 1+2+3. Unchanged.
135	6. **Adversarial code review on every story, with explicit Patch / Defer / Dismiss triage.** 9-for-9. Unchanged. Post-merge code-review-on-fresh-context + follow-up hardening PR is now a validated pattern (PR #194).
136	7. **When a downstream story's `Given` premise breaks mid-epic, patch the downstream planning artifact in the current story's review.** Epic 2 pattern; held in Epic 3 (Story 3.1 review → Story 3.3 scope; Story 3.2 HIGH → Story 3.3 Patch C).
137	8. **NEW: ACs that contain inline scripts (awk, sed, bash pipelines) MUST be dry-run at story-authoring time, not only at dev-agent time.** Story 3.3 Patch C awk was literally wrong in the spec; dev-agent local dry-run caught it. If it had gone direct to CI on the first `main`-branch release cut, a malformed CHANGELOG would have shipped to npm in v1.0.0's permanent history.
138	9. **NEW: Task 1 recon probes MUST verify AC-encoded claims about API semantics, check-names, ruleset IDs, and env behavior against live state.** Story 3.3 AC #5 and AC #6 both drifted; recon would have surfaced both pre-execution.
139	10. **NEW: Never write `AC #N` in commit bodies.** Use `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N`. The `conventionalcommits` preset interprets any `#N` as a GitHub issue reference and emits `closes [#N]` links that pollute the immutable CHANGELOG entry.
140	11. **NEW: `HUSKY: "0"` at job-level env** is the pattern for any GitHub Actions workflow whose bot commits land on runners without the maintainer's local `entire` tool. Pair with (F4) defense-in-depth (`-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null`) if the env is ever split across matrix cells or reusable workflows.
141	
142	### Standing flags (no fire date)
143	
144	12. **Flip `prevent_self_review: false → true` on the `release` environment AND revoke the ruleset's solo-maintainer bypass-list entry** (`RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request`) when a second reviewer is onboarded. Epic 1 carry-forward, now clarified per Story 3.3 AC #6 drift — both the env flag and the ruleset bypass need to flip together, not just one.
145	13. **`install.js:23` silent exit 0 on `{success: false}`** contradicts STABILITY.md. Deferred to Story 5.1 pre-flight; revisit if any pre-v1.0.0 work touches that file.
146	14. **`package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint.** Deferred to Story 5.1 pre-flight.
147	15. **Orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` (Story 3.2 alpha-cut CI-ephemeral) is reachable only via tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` + branch `feat/release-workflow`.** Do NOT delete the branch until Story 5.4 closes (v1.0.0 shipped) AND GitHub retention behavior on orphaned commits is confirmed. Story 6-adjacent cleanup story is the natural home for branch deletion.
148	
149	## Readiness assessment
150	
151	| Dimension | Status |
152	|-----------|--------|
153	| Story completion | ✅ 3/3 done |
154	| Live artifacts captured | ✅ commits `1295bf6`/`34ad130`, `cbc7851` + `de2334b` + `cae5861` + `535e38f`, `ab74c10` + `76a62cd` + `10c1134` + `6a6a521`; merge-commits `14cedfe` (PR #193) + `b662bce` (PR #194) |
155	| Alpha cut live on npm | ✅ `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` under `--tag alpha` with SLSA-L2 provenance verified |
156	| `release.yaml` on `main` | ✅ 254 lines; canonical OIDC path |
157	| Legacy workflows inert | ✅ `publish.yaml` `v*` trigger neutralized; both have DEPRECATED headers |
158	| CHANGELOG preamble-restore hardened | ✅ 4 code-review guards landed in PR #194 |
159	| Adversarial code review | ✅ all three stories + post-merge hardening loop |
160	| Deferred work tracked | ✅ `deferred-work.md` current (~30 items across Epics 1–3) |
161	| Unresolved blockers for Epic 4 start | none — F1–F6, G1–G4, H1–H9, I1–I5 are inputs for Story 5.1 audit or post-v1.0.0, NOT blockers for Story 4.1/4.2 |
162	| Scope envelope adherence | ✅ 3/3 stories held to file-list AC |
163	| Feature branch preservation | ✅ `feat/release-workflow` on origin at `6a6a521` (NFR12 reversibility boundary intact) |
164	| Epic status | already flipped `in-progress` → `done` at Story 3.3 close-out |
165	
166	Epic 3 is truly done. The canonical OIDC release path is on `main`, operationally proven via a live alpha cut with verifiable SLSA-L2 provenance. Epic 4 (docs-only: rollback playbook + deprecation prose) can start whenever.
167	
168	## Next steps
169	
170	1. Epic 4 planning via `bmad-create-story` for Story 4.1 when ready — import commitments 1–4 above into Dev Notes at create-story phase.
171	2. Story 4.2 scope confirmed narrowed (no workflow-header re-edit).
172	3. Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit inherits items H1–H9, plus decide the disposition of F1–F6 and G1–G4 at audit time.
173	4. No epic-update review session needed — nothing in Epic 3 invalidates Epic 4, Epic 5, or Epic 6's current definitions. Epic 4 is still doc-only per `epics.md`; workflow hardening remains queued for Story 5.1 or post-v1.0.0 per the original sequencing.

# Epic 3 Retrospective — Consolidated OIDC Release Workflow

**Date:** 2026-04-21
**Facilitator:** bmad-retrospective (Amelia)
**Project Lead:** Armel
**Epic status at retro:** all 3 stories `done`; epic already flipped to `done` at Story 3.3 close-out; retrospective key `epic-3-retrospective` flipped `optional` → `done` as part of this retro.
**Previous retrospective:** Epic 2 (2026-04-21).

## Summary

Epic 3 consolidated `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` + local `release:*` scripts into a single canonical `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (254 lines on `main`) using OIDC Trusted Publishing with auto-provenance (SLSA Build Level 2), reviewer-gated `release` Environment, concurrency protection, dist-tag routing, CHANGELOG append with preamble-restore, and full pre-publish validation. Proven live: `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` published to npm with verified provenance attestation via Story 3.2's alpha cut. Legacy workflows retained in-repo as inert backup — `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger neutralized, both legacy files carry `DEPRECATED` headers. All three stories landed on **2026-04-21**.

Delivered via two PRs to `main`:

- **PR #193** merge-commit `14cedfe` — consolidated feature branch (23 commits) including Story 3.1 (commit `1295bf6` + 5 review patches → `34ad130`), Story 3.2 (4 load-bearing defect-fixes on `release.yaml` + 1 superseded-but-preserved + 1 net-zero temp-trigger pair), Story 3.2 review patch (`535e38f` npm floor assertion), Story 3.3 Patches A/B/C (`ab74c10`, `76a62cd`, `10c1134`).
- **PR #194** merge-commit `b662bce` — Story 3.3 code-review hardening patches (`6a6a521`) on Patch C's awk preamble-restore step.

| Story | Outcome | Review result |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| 3.1 Author canonical `release.yaml` (`1295bf6` + `34ad130`) | 174-line workflow greenfield-created on `feat/release-workflow`; `[Unreleased]` anchor pre-inserted; Windows-cell `conventional-changelog-cli` smoke added to `quality.yaml`; file NOT merged (Story 3.3 territory) | 3 decision-needed + 3 patch + 5 defer; Acceptance Auditor all-green on ACs #1–#18 |
| 3.2 Validate `release.yaml` via alpha cut (4 defect-fix commits + `535e38f`) | Run `24714953668` succeeded on 5th attempt; `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` published under `--tag alpha` with `attestations.provenance.predicateType = https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1`; `dist-tags.latest` unchanged at `0.10.0`; GitHub Release `v0.10.1-alpha.0` created with `prerelease: true`; allow-list widen/revoke cycle clean; **HIGH finding: CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle structurally broken** by preset's byte-0 prepend | 1 HIGH (deferred to 3.3) + 2 MED + 3 LOW + 2 INFO dev-surfaced + 1 patch (`535e38f`) + 6 defer from formal review |
| 3.3 Merge `release.yaml` to `main` (`ab74c10` + `76a62cd` + `10c1134` + `6a6a521`) | Patch A (neutralize `publish.yaml` `v*` trigger) + Patch B (DEPRECATED headers on both legacy files) + Patch C (CHANGELOG preamble-restore awk step) landed atomically in PR #193; post-merge code review found 4 Patch C hardening gaps (empty-file guard, anchored regex, `seen_title` assertion, post-awk empty-output check) fixed in PR #194 | 4 code-review patches (1 HIGH + 3 MED) all applied; AC drift flagged on AC #5 (stale check names) and AC #6 (GitHub self-approval semantics) |

Commits on `feat/release-workflow` for Epic 3: ~13 spanning Stories 3.1–3.3 plus the code-review hardening commit on the follow-up PR. Feature branch preserved post-merge at `6a6a521` (NFR12 reversibility boundary — alpha tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` points at orphaned CI-ephemeral commit `2a57dcbd` reachable only via the tag + branch ancestry chain).

## What went well

1. **Epic 2 retro carry-forwards landed 6-for-6.** (A) tooling — stayed on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` with rationale captured in commit body; (B) `[Unreleased]` anchor pre-inserted at Story 3.1 Task 4; (C) bogus-issue-ref bash guard added and later widened to `(closes|fixes|resolves)` in Story 3.1 code review; (D) `.yaml` vs `.yml` triple-guard held — and a bonus fourth guard surfaced: ESLint `yml/file-extension` rule (`eslint.config.mjs:65-72`) enforces `.yaml` at lint time; (E) Windows-cell smoke step added to `quality.yaml`'s `validate` job; Story 3.3 precondition — atomic neutralization of `publish.yaml` `v*` tag trigger — bundled into PR #193 as Patch A. Nothing orphaned from Epic 2 retro.
2. **Local dry-run caught an AC-literal bug at authoring-time — new "dry-run gate" pattern validated.** Story 3.3 AC #3.C.i's verbatim awk would have produced `# Changelog → preamble → new release → [Unreleased] → older` (inverting the anchor/new-release pair vs. the Keep-a-Changelog shape the *same* AC stated in prose). Dev-agent Task 5 dry-run on `git show v0.10.1-alpha.0:CHANGELOG.md` as test fixture found the bug; one-line fix (removed the erroneous `section==1 && /^## \[Unreleased\]/ { section=2 }` rule); stayed in approach (C.i), no escalation to Python helper needed. Lesson for future stories: **ACs that contain inline scripts MUST be dry-run at story-authoring time, not only at dev-agent time.**
3. **OIDC chain worked end-to-end against real infrastructure.** Run `24714953668` — `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (no token fallback), no `--provenance` flag (auto under OIDC), `.yaml` filename matched TP registration. `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0 --json` showed `attestations.provenance.predicateType = https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1`; `npm audit signatures` on a fresh install reported verified attestation. NFR4 (100% provenance) operationally satisfied for the first time.
4. **Audit-trail discipline held under 4 iterations of defect-fixes.** Story 3.2 burned 4 failed alpha-cut runs before succeeding. Zero amends, zero force-pushes — even the superseded `f449c67` (first `--force` attempt that didn't work, replaced by `cbc7851` side-prefix install) kept in history. The commit ledger (`e470501 → f449c67 → cbc7851 → de2334b → cae5861 → 24714953668 green → 7208ff4 revert`) reads as a linear narrative of the debugging path. This is AC #11 "no amend, no force-push" being load-bearing for post-incident forensics.
5. **Forward-flagging + post-merge hardening discipline.** Story 3.1 review patched Story 3.3's scope (Patches A/B/C bundled from three separate deferred-work entries spanning Epic 1 + Epic 2 + Story 3.1 + Story 3.2). Story 3.2's HIGH `[Unreleased]`-lifecycle finding flowed straight into Story 3.3 Patch C at create-story phase. After Story 3.3 merge, `bmad-code-review` on fresh context surfaced four edge-case vulnerabilities in Patch C's awk (empty CHANGELOG.md, prefix-matching title header, embedded `# Changelog ...` in commit bodies, awk exit-zero-with-empty-output) — all fixed in PR #194 (`6a6a521`) before any future release could hit them.
6. **Scope envelope held across all three stories.** Each story's file-list AC enumerated forbidden files; every commit diff matched expectation. No drive-by `release:*` script edits, no `package.json` drive-bys, no `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook` prose pre-emption (Story 4.1 territory), no `CONTRIBUTING.md` edits (Story 4.2 territory).

## Patterns across all three stories

### Preset-behavior assumptions cannot be validated by hand-reading — only by running the preset

Story 3.1 pre-inserted `## [Unreleased]` expecting `conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` to honor the anchor as the insertion point. It doesn't — the preset prepends at byte-0 of the infile unconditionally, pushing `# Changelog` title + preamble + `[Unreleased]` anchor DOWN below the new entry. Only Story 3.2's concrete alpha cut exposed this; the file-ordering damage compounds with every release cut. Story 3.3 Patch C (awk preamble-restore) remediated it. **Generalization:** when a story design rests on assumed third-party tool behavior, the alpha/smoke cut IS the validator — accept that upfront, plan the fix into the downstream story, don't wait for post-hoc surprise.

### Asymmetric error-handling in husky hooks is a CI-gotcha class-of-bug

Story 3.2's `Commit version bump` failure traced to `.husky/commit-msg` using strict `|| exit 1` when invoking the local-only `entire` command, while sibling hooks (`post-commit`, `prepare-commit-msg`, `pre-push`) gracefully degrade via `|| true`. Only `commit-msg` hard-blocks CI. Fix: `HUSKY: "0"` at job level (husky's documented opt-out). Defense-in-depth recommendation tracked in deferred-work: belt-and-braces add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call so a future matrix split or reusable-workflow extraction that drops the env doesn't silently reintroduce the failure.

### Dev-agent commit-body `AC #N` → preset parses `#N` as GH issue ref

Four Story 3.2 defect-fix commit bodies referenced `AC #11` / `AC #12` meaning Acceptance Criteria. The `conventionalcommits` preset interpreted these as GitHub issue references and emitted `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` links in the CHANGELOG for `0.10.1-alpha.0`. The bogus-issue-ref guard correctly did NOT fire (refs are numeric; guard targets the Story 2.2 class-of-bug of non-numeric refs). Cosmetic pollution in the immutable alpha CHANGELOG entry, not blocking. **Process fix for all future dev agents:** never write `AC #N` in commit bodies — write `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N`. `#N` is reserved for genuine GitHub issue refs.

### AC drift surfaced at Story 3.3 execution — Task 1 recon should probe for it

Story 3.3 AC #5 enumerated seven `quality.yaml` required status check names (`quality.yaml / schemas|refs|tests`); actual ruleset `13855503` names are `prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)`. Same coverage shape, different job IDs — story text drifted. Story 3.3 AC #6 assumed `prevent_self_review: false` on the `release` environment permits self-approval on one's own PR. **Wrong semantic:** GitHub's API rejects self-approval on one's own PR *unconditionally*, regardless of env-level `prevent_self_review`. The solo-maintainer path is admin-bypass via the ruleset's bypass-list (`RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request`), which ruleset `13855503` was already designed with — but the story text didn't know. **Generalization:** hard-coded check names, ruleset/env IDs, and API-semantic assumptions must be verified against live state at Task 1 recon — not trusted from the AC.

### `--force` doesn't unblock an in-flight arborist rebuild race

Story 3.2 `f449c67` tried `npm install -g npm@latest --force` to dodge a self-upgrade race on the GitHub-hosted runner's bundled npm. The `--force` flag didn't help because the race is about `rebuild.js` reading `promise-retry` from a now-partial `lib/node_modules/npm/` tree mid-install. `cbc7851` superseded with a side-prefix install (`$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp` + `$GITHUB_PATH` prepend) — the old npm's tree stays intact, subsequent steps resolve to the new npm. Plus `535e38f` (added post-review) now asserts the `$GITHUB_PATH` propagation actually worked by running bare `npm --version` and comparing against a `sort -V`-based `11.5.1` floor. Root-cause understanding matters — the shortcut flag didn't address the actual race.

## Load-bearing inputs carried into Epic 4 and beyond

Epic 4 is planned doc-only per `epics.md` (Story 4.1 rollback playbook + Story 4.2 CONTRIBUTING.md deprecation prose + `release:*` script fail-loud prefixes). The following items are NOT folded into Epic 4 — they're queued for Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit or post-v1.0.0 hardening per the current epic sequencing. `deferred-work.md` is the source of truth; each item below has a named home.

### Release-workflow hardening (Story 5.1 audit or post-v1.0.0)

**(F1) Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` classification + empty-`$VERSION` → `@latest` fallback.** `release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish) — both chains. Future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain; empty `$VERSION` routes to `@latest` silently. Fix: semver-aware parsing or anchoring to prerelease identifier segment + `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]] && exit 1` guard on both chains. Tracked from Story 3.2 code review; doubled via dry-run mirror in commit `de2334b`.

**(F2) Tag-push-before-npm-publish ordering.** `release.yaml:161-181`. OIDC failure after tag-push orphans `vX.Y.Z` on `origin` without an npm artifact. Story 4.1 Scenario E (manual recovery) documents the prose fix; structural fix (tag-after-publish reorder or compensating tag-delete on publish failure) belongs with a dedicated hardening story post-v1.0.0.

**(F3) Zero-conventional-commit release path.** `release.yaml:119` Update CHANGELOG step — preset prepends nothing, Patch C's preamble-restore correctly no-ops, but workflow still proceeds to tag + publish with no CHANGELOG entry. Candidate for a post-v1.0.0 release-safety hardening pass — detect empty prepend and `exit 1`.

**(F4) Husky defense-in-depth.** `HUSKY: "0"` at job level is the sole safety net. A matrix split or reusable-workflow extraction could drop the env and silently reintroduce Story 3.2's `commit-msg` failure. Belt-and-braces: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` invocation in the `Commit version bump` step.

**(F5) Non-conventional commit prefix `release:`.** `release.yaml:125`. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat|fix|chore|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|revert`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless today under `HUSKY=0`, doubles the failure surface if that env is ever dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):` — bundle with (F4) defense-in-depth.

**(F6) Feature-branch alpha-cut tags orphan on branch deletion.** Tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` points at ephemeral commit `2a57dcbd` reachable only via the tag + `feat/release-workflow` ancestry. Post-merge branch deletion (common habit from other PR workflows) + GitHub retention GC could break `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` in future runs. Story 3.3 AC #10 enforces branch preservation; longer-term structural fix (cherry-pick alpha-cut commits to `main` post-validation, or change workflow to require `main` dispatch) belongs with Story 4.1 rollback semantics or a dedicated hardening story.

### Post-v1.0.0 supply-chain hardening (dedicated story or Story 5.1)

**(G1) SHA-pin third-party actions.** `release.yaml` uses `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4` — floating major-version tags. With `id-token: write` + `contents: write`, a compromised maintainer or compromised tag could exfiltrate. Industry best practice. Consistent with repo-wide patterns elsewhere, so not a regression — but worth a dedicated supply-chain pass post-v1.0.0.

**(G2) `npm install -g npm@latest` unpinned.** `release.yaml:52`. Future `npm@12.x` with breaking OIDC-surface changes would silently break the next release. `535e38f` now asserts floor `≥11.5.1` post-install, but does not prevent a major-version break. Patch-grade fix: `npm@^11.5.1`.

**(G3) Node 20 deprecation warnings.** Repo-wide — fires on every `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`. GitHub forcing Node 24 by 2026-06-02; Node 20 removed 2026-09-16. Two paths: rely on action maintainers to publish Node-24 versions (most have), or set `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` at workflow level now. Post-v1.0.0 maintenance story.

**(G4) `uv` cache-miss warning on every release run.** `release.yaml:54-57`. `setup-uv` looks for `requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml` / `uv.lock`; none present so cache never fills. Low-priority cleanup — add a Python `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock` or silence via `cache-dependency-glob`.

### Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit backlog (inherited + new from Epic 3)

**(H1)** `commands/install.js:23` silent `exit 0` on `{success: false}` — contract gap with STABILITY.md's "`skf install` SHALL exit 1 on unhandled failure" (Epic 2 carry-over, still unresolved).

**(H2)** `package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint (Epic 2 carry-over, still unresolved).

**(H3)** `marketplace.json` unanchored sed fragility if schema grows a second `"version"` field (Story 3.1 known-fragility, acknowledged in spec).

**(H4)** Starlight sidebar entry missing for `docs/STABILITY.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` (Epic 1 + Epic 2 carry-over).

**(H5)** Hand-curate v1.0.0 CHANGELOG from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` workshop file (Epic 2 carry-over; Story 5.1 owns the final prose).

**(H6)** DRAFT → STABLE banner flip on `docs/STABILITY.md` (Epic 2 carry-over; Story 5.1 owns).

**(H7)** `0.10.1-alpha.0` CHANGELOG's `closes [#11]` / `[#12]` cosmetic refs from dev-agent commit-body `AC #N` pollution — decide: hand-correct during Story 5.1 audit, or accept as historical artifact (alpha is explicit throwaway per Story 3.2 AC #9).

**(H8)** One-time hand-bump path `0.10.0 → 1.0.0-rc.0` before `version_bump: rc` for Story 5.2. `npm version prerelease --preid=rc` on `0.10.0` produces `0.10.1-rc.0`, not `1.0.0-rc.0`. Procedure: `npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version` on a pre-cut commit to `main` via PR, then dispatch `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc` to advance to `1.0.0-rc.1`. Story 5.1 documents this in `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`.

**(H9)** `prevent_self_review: false → true` flip on the `release` environment when a second reviewer is onboarded. Epic 1 standing flag; now clarified per Story 3.3 AC #6 drift — this is about *ruleset bypass-list revoke* (remove `RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request` bypass) in addition to the env-level flag flip. Full solo-maintainer-workflow-to-multi-reviewer transition needs both.

### CI-as-enforced-gate candidates (standalone story post-v1.0.0 or Story 5.1)

Carry-over from Epic 1 + Epic 2 retros — prose-only constraints that CI could enforce:

**(I1)** `lychee CHANGELOG.md` or `markdown-link-check` CI step scoped to the changelog — regression guard against Story 2.2's `closes [#F59E0B]` class-of-bug.

**(I2)** `release` env zero-secrets assertion — `gh api /environments/release/secrets` must return `total_count: 0`, fail CI otherwise.

**(I3)** Name-based lookup instead of hard-coded ruleset `13855503` / environment `14347249917` IDs in `docs/RELEASING.md`.

**(I4)** Trusted Publisher registration shape vs repo reality assertion — CI reads `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` table and verifies `.github/workflows/release.yaml` exists, environment `release` exists, `package.json` name matches.

**(I5)** `package.json.devDependencies` mirror-entry drift guard in `package-lock.json.packages[""].devDependencies` — lightweight hygiene (Epic 2 carry-over).

### No Story 4.2 / Story 3.3 overlap cleanup needed

Story 3.3 Patch B shipped the DEPRECATED comment header wording on both `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` verbatim matching Story 4.2's AC reference text. Story 4.2 scope narrows to CONTRIBUTING.md "Releasing" section prose + `release:*` script fail-loud prefixes in `package.json`. No re-edit of workflow headers needed.

## Commitments

### Carry-forward into Epic 4

1. **Story 4.1 `create-story` MUST import `deferred-work.md § Story 3.1/3.2/3.3 code review` entries for Scenarios D/E/F.** Scenario E (npm publish succeeded but tag/push failed) has a concrete answer from Story 3.2's observed ordering (tag pushed before publish — the risk is inverted from the AC's premise). Scenario D (tag exists but publish failed) maps directly to Story 3.2's orphaned-commit pattern. Scenario F (OIDC compromise) references Story 1.3's TP registration surface.
2. **Story 4.1 MUST add a "release.yaml disabled / reverted" scenario** per Story 3.3 code-review defer. Low probability, high-consequence fallback gap when `release.yaml` is the single release root.
3. **Story 4.2 `create-story` MUST NOT re-edit the legacy workflow headers** — Story 3.3 Patch B already landed them verbatim. Scope narrows to CONTRIBUTING.md + `package.json release:*` prefixes.
4. **Story 4.1 MUST use name-based lookups** (`gh api .../rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id'`) NOT hard-coded ruleset/env IDs in any new prose — Epic 1 code-review defer carries over.

### Process improvements (continue across Epic 4+)

5. **Live `gh api` / UI recon block at Task 1 of every story.** 9-for-9 across Epics 1+2+3. Unchanged.
6. **Adversarial code review on every story, with explicit Patch / Defer / Dismiss triage.** 9-for-9. Unchanged. Post-merge code-review-on-fresh-context + follow-up hardening PR is now a validated pattern (PR #194).
7. **When a downstream story's `Given` premise breaks mid-epic, patch the downstream planning artifact in the current story's review.** Epic 2 pattern; held in Epic 3 (Story 3.1 review → Story 3.3 scope; Story 3.2 HIGH → Story 3.3 Patch C).
8. **NEW: ACs that contain inline scripts (awk, sed, bash pipelines) MUST be dry-run at story-authoring time, not only at dev-agent time.** Story 3.3 Patch C awk was literally wrong in the spec; dev-agent local dry-run caught it. If it had gone direct to CI on the first `main`-branch release cut, a malformed CHANGELOG would have shipped to npm in v1.0.0's permanent history.
9. **NEW: Task 1 recon probes MUST verify AC-encoded claims about API semantics, check-names, ruleset IDs, and env behavior against live state.** Story 3.3 AC #5 and AC #6 both drifted; recon would have surfaced both pre-execution.
10. **NEW: Never write `AC #N` in commit bodies.** Use `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N`. The `conventionalcommits` preset interprets any `#N` as a GitHub issue reference and emits `closes [#N]` links that pollute the immutable CHANGELOG entry.
11. **NEW: `HUSKY: "0"` at job-level env** is the pattern for any GitHub Actions workflow whose bot commits land on runners without the maintainer's local `entire` tool. Pair with (F4) defense-in-depth (`-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null`) if the env is ever split across matrix cells or reusable workflows.

### Standing flags (no fire date)

12. **Flip `prevent_self_review: false → true` on the `release` environment AND revoke the ruleset's solo-maintainer bypass-list entry** (`RepositoryRole 5, mode: pull_request`) when a second reviewer is onboarded. Epic 1 carry-forward, now clarified per Story 3.3 AC #6 drift — both the env flag and the ruleset bypass need to flip together, not just one.
13. **`install.js:23` silent exit 0 on `{success: false}`** contradicts STABILITY.md. Deferred to Story 5.1 pre-flight; revisit if any pre-v1.0.0 work touches that file.
14. **`package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint.** Deferred to Story 5.1 pre-flight.
15. **Orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` (Story 3.2 alpha-cut CI-ephemeral) is reachable only via tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` + branch `feat/release-workflow`.** Do NOT delete the branch until Story 5.4 closes (v1.0.0 shipped) AND GitHub retention behavior on orphaned commits is confirmed. Story 6-adjacent cleanup story is the natural home for branch deletion.

## Readiness assessment

| Dimension | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| Story completion | ✅ 3/3 done |
| Live artifacts captured | ✅ commits `1295bf6`/`34ad130`, `cbc7851` + `de2334b` + `cae5861` + `535e38f`, `ab74c10` + `76a62cd` + `10c1134` + `6a6a521`; merge-commits `14cedfe` (PR #193) + `b662bce` (PR #194) |
| Alpha cut live on npm | ✅ `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` under `--tag alpha` with SLSA-L2 provenance verified |
| `release.yaml` on `main` | ✅ 254 lines; canonical OIDC path |
| Legacy workflows inert | ✅ `publish.yaml` `v*` trigger neutralized; both have DEPRECATED headers |
| CHANGELOG preamble-restore hardened | ✅ 4 code-review guards landed in PR #194 |
| Adversarial code review | ✅ all three stories + post-merge hardening loop |
| Deferred work tracked | ✅ `deferred-work.md` current (~30 items across Epics 1–3) |
| Unresolved blockers for Epic 4 start | none — F1–F6, G1–G4, H1–H9, I1–I5 are inputs for Story 5.1 audit or post-v1.0.0, NOT blockers for Story 4.1/4.2 |
| Scope envelope adherence | ✅ 3/3 stories held to file-list AC |
| Feature branch preservation | ✅ `feat/release-workflow` on origin at `6a6a521` (NFR12 reversibility boundary intact) |
| Epic status | already flipped `in-progress` → `done` at Story 3.3 close-out |

Epic 3 is truly done. The canonical OIDC release path is on `main`, operationally proven via a live alpha cut with verifiable SLSA-L2 provenance. Epic 4 (docs-only: rollback playbook + deprecation prose) can start whenever.

## Next steps

1. Epic 4 planning via `bmad-create-story` for Story 4.1 when ready — import commitments 1–4 above into Dev Notes at create-story phase.
2. Story 4.2 scope confirmed narrowed (no workflow-header re-edit).
3. Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit inherits items H1–H9, plus decide the disposition of F1–F6 and G1–G4 at audit time.
4. No epic-update review session needed — nothing in Epic 3 invalidates Epic 4, Epic 5, or Epic 6's current definitions. Epic 4 is still doc-only per `epics.md`; workflow hardening remains queued for Story 5.1 or post-v1.0.0 per the original sequencing.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

5	# Story 3.1: Tiered Quality Gate — Hard Gate
6	
7	Status: done
8	
9	## Story
10	
11	As a skill author,
12	I want the test pipeline to block on unresolved critical/high findings before scoring,
13	So that no skill with serious defects can pass regardless of its coverage percentage.
14	
15	## Acceptance Criteria
16	
17	1. **Given** a skill with one or more unresolved critical-severity findings
18	   **When** TS runs the hard gate check (`step-hard-gate.md`) before scoring
19	   **Then** the pipeline blocks with a clear message listing each critical/high finding
20	   **And** no coverage score is computed or reported
21	
22	2. **Given** a skill with one or more unresolved high-severity findings
23	   **When** TS runs the hard gate check
24	   **Then** the pipeline blocks with the same behavior as critical findings
25	
26	3. **Given** a skill with only medium and low severity findings
27	   **When** TS runs the hard gate check
28	   **Then** the hard gate passes and the pipeline proceeds to the soft gate (coverage scoring)
29	   **And** medium/low findings are documented in the gap report but do not block
30	
31	## Tasks / Subtasks
32	
33	- [x] Task 1: Create `step-hard-gate.md` step file (AC: #1, #2, #3)
34	  - [x] 1.1 Write frontmatter with `nextStepFile: 'score.md'` and `outputFile` reference
35	  - [x] 1.2 Implement step goal section: scan accumulated findings for critical/high severity
36	  - [x] 1.3 Implement findings parsing — read the Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis sections from `{outputFile}` and extract severity from each GAP entry
37	  - [x] 1.4 Implement blocking behavior — on critical/high: HALT with a clear message listing every blocking finding (title, severity, source), set `testResult: 'fail'` in output frontmatter, update `stepsCompleted`
38	  - [x] 1.5 Implement pass-through behavior — on no critical/high findings: log "Hard gate passed — N medium/low findings noted, proceeding to scoring", update `stepsCompleted`, chain to `score.md`
39	  - [x] 1.6 Implement headless envelope emission on block — emit `SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON` to stderr with `status: "error"`, `verdict: "FAIL"`, `halt_reason: "hard-gate-blocked"`, `exit_code: 2`
40	  - [x] 1.7 Add `stepsCompleted` update (`'hard-gate'`) before chaining to next step

Decision: the hard gate does NOT write a new section to the report — it only reads existing findings and decides pass/block. The anchor mapping comment should be updated to note the hard gate's role but no new `## Hard Gate` section is needed.

53	
54	### Integration Point — Step Chain

56	The TS workflow steps chain via `nextStepFile` frontmatter. Current chain:
57

60	```

66	```

71	
72	### How Findings Are Structured in the Output File
73	
74	Coverage-check and coherence-check write gap entries into the output file in this format:
75	
76	```markdown
77	### GAP-{NNN}: {Brief title}
78	
79	**Severity:** {Critical|High|Medium|Low|Info}
80	**Category:** {Coverage|Coherence|Structural}
81	**Source:** {file:line or section reference}
82	
83	**Issue:** {description}
84	
85	**Remediation:** {action}
86	```
87	
88	The hard gate step must parse the output file (`{outputFile}`) looking for lines matching `**Severity:** Critical` or `**Severity:** High`. It does NOT need to understand the full gap structure — just scan for severity markers.
89	
90	### Severity Definitions (from scoring-rules.md)
91	
92	| Severity | Criteria |
93	|----------|----------|
94	| Critical | Missing exported function/class documentation |
95	| High | Signature mismatch between source and SKILL.md |
96	| Medium | Missing type or interface documentation |
97	| Low | Missing optional metadata or examples |
98	| Info | Style suggestions, non-blocking observations |
99	
100	The hard gate blocks on **Critical** and **High** only. Medium, Low, and Info pass through.
101	
102	### Step File Conventions (from architecture + existing steps)
103	
104	- Frontmatter: `nextStepFile`, `outputFile` (match existing steps)
105	- Section heading: `## STEP GOAL:` (TS convention — not `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE`)
106	- Update `stepsCompleted` in output file frontmatter before chaining
107	- Communicate in `{communication_language}`
108	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with default action and log each auto-decision
109	- Zero hallucination — every finding must trace to actual code with file:line citations
110	
111	### Headless Envelope on Hard Gate Block
112	
113	When the hard gate blocks in headless mode, emit to **stderr**:
114	
115	```
116	SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","skill_name":"…","verdict":"FAIL","score":null,"threshold":null,"report_path":"…","next_workflow":"update-skill","exit_code":2,"halt_reason":"hard-gate-blocked"}
117	```
118	
119	This follows the existing HALT envelope pattern from SKILL.md's Result Contract section. Note `score: null` because scoring was never reached. `exit_code: 2` per the exit code table (fail verdict). Add `"hard-gate-blocked"` to the `halt_reason` enum in SKILL.md's Result Contract documentation.
120	
121	### SKILL.md Stages Table Update
122	
123	Insert the hard gate as step 4c (between External Validators 4b and Score 5):
124	
125	```
126	| 4c | Hard Gate | references/step-hard-gate.md | Yes |
127	```
128	
129	Auto-proceed = Yes because the hard gate is deterministic (scan findings, block or pass — no user input needed).
130	
131	### What Must NOT Change
132	
133	- `score.md` — no modifications; the scoring step continues to work exactly as-is
134	- `coverage-check.md` — no modifications; findings continue to be written in the existing format
135	- `coherence-check.md` — no modifications; findings continue to be written in the existing format
136	- `report.md` — no modifications; gap report section is populated after scoring
137	- The gap entry format — hard gate reads the existing format, does not change it
138	- Exit codes 0, 3, 4 — these remain for score.md/report.md. The hard gate uses exit code 2 (fail) on block.
139	
140	### Hard Gate Does NOT Score
141	
142	The hard gate is a binary pass/block check. It does not compute a coverage percentage, does not use the `compute-score.py` script, and does not reference the `min:N` threshold. It only examines finding severity.
143	
144	### Project Structure Notes

146	- New file path: `src/skf-test-skill/references/step-hard-gate.md` — matches the architecture FR→File mapping (FR-11)
147	- No new Python scripts needed — the hard gate is implemented entirely as a step file (LLM judgment on finding severity markers)
148	- Structural integration tests added in `test/test-skf-step-hard-gate.py` (35+ test cases covering pipeline chain, frontmatter, structure, severity scanning, block/pass paths, headless envelope, stages table, exit codes, result contract, and stepsCompleted integration)
149	
150	### Epic 2 Retro Learnings (Apply These)

152	1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
153	2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After changing `external-validators.md` frontmatter, grep for any other files that reference `external-validators.md`'s `nextStepFile` or assume `score.md` follows `external-validators.md`
154	3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections within the step file (§1, §2, etc.) as established in existing TS step files

# Story 3.1: Tiered Quality Gate — Hard Gate

Status: done

## Story

As a skill author,
I want the test pipeline to block on unresolved critical/high findings before scoring,
So that no skill with serious defects can pass regardless of its coverage percentage.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a skill with one or more unresolved critical-severity findings
   **When** TS runs the hard gate check (`step-hard-gate.md`) before scoring
   **Then** the pipeline blocks with a clear message listing each critical/high finding
   **And** no coverage score is computed or reported

2. **Given** a skill with one or more unresolved high-severity findings
   **When** TS runs the hard gate check
   **Then** the pipeline blocks with the same behavior as critical findings

3. **Given** a skill with only medium and low severity findings
   **When** TS runs the hard gate check
   **Then** the hard gate passes and the pipeline proceeds to the soft gate (coverage scoring)
   **And** medium/low findings are documented in the gap report but do not block

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create `step-hard-gate.md` step file (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 1.1 Write frontmatter with `nextStepFile: 'score.md'` and `outputFile` reference
  - [x] 1.2 Implement step goal section: scan accumulated findings for critical/high severity
  - [x] 1.3 Implement findings parsing — read the Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis sections from `{outputFile}` and extract severity from each GAP entry
  - [x] 1.4 Implement blocking behavior — on critical/high: HALT with a clear message listing every blocking finding (title, severity, source), set `testResult: 'fail'` in output frontmatter, update `stepsCompleted`
  - [x] 1.5 Implement pass-through behavior — on no critical/high findings: log "Hard gate passed — N medium/low findings noted, proceeding to scoring", update `stepsCompleted`, chain to `score.md`
  - [x] 1.6 Implement headless envelope emission on block — emit `SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON` to stderr with `status: "error"`, `verdict: "FAIL"`, `halt_reason: "hard-gate-blocked"`, `exit_code: 2`
  - [x] 1.7 Add `stepsCompleted` update (`'hard-gate'`) before chaining to next step

- [x] Task 2: Modify `external-validators.md` frontmatter (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 2.1 Change `nextStepFile` from `'score.md'` to `'step-hard-gate.md'`

- [x] Task 3: Update SKILL.md stages table (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 3.1 Insert hard gate row between step 4b (External Validators) and step 5 (Score)
  - [x] 3.2 Add exit code for hard-gate-blocked halt (exit 2 with `halt_reason: "hard-gate-blocked"`)

- [x] Task 4: Update test report template anchors (AC: #1)
  - [x] 4.1 Evaluate whether the test report template (`templates/test-report-template.md`) needs a new anchor comment for the hard gate step. Decision: the hard gate does NOT write a new section to the report — it only reads existing findings and decides pass/block. The anchor mapping comment should be updated to note the hard gate's role but no new `## Hard Gate` section is needed.

## Dev Notes

### Integration Point — Step Chain

The TS workflow steps chain via `nextStepFile` frontmatter. Current chain:

```
init.md → detect-mode.md → coverage-check.md → coherence-check.md → external-validators.md → score.md → report.md → health-check.md
```

After this story:

```
init.md → detect-mode.md → coverage-check.md → coherence-check.md → external-validators.md → step-hard-gate.md → score.md → report.md → health-check.md
```

Two files modified:
1. `external-validators.md` line 2: `nextStepFile: 'score.md'` → `nextStepFile: 'step-hard-gate.md'`
2. New file `step-hard-gate.md` with `nextStepFile: 'score.md'`

### How Findings Are Structured in the Output File

Coverage-check and coherence-check write gap entries into the output file in this format:

```markdown
### GAP-{NNN}: {Brief title}

**Severity:** {Critical|High|Medium|Low|Info}
**Category:** {Coverage|Coherence|Structural}
**Source:** {file:line or section reference}

**Issue:** {description}

**Remediation:** {action}
```

The hard gate step must parse the output file (`{outputFile}`) looking for lines matching `**Severity:** Critical` or `**Severity:** High`. It does NOT need to understand the full gap structure — just scan for severity markers.

### Severity Definitions (from scoring-rules.md)

| Severity | Criteria |
|----------|----------|
| Critical | Missing exported function/class documentation |
| High | Signature mismatch between source and SKILL.md |
| Medium | Missing type or interface documentation |
| Low | Missing optional metadata or examples |
| Info | Style suggestions, non-blocking observations |

The hard gate blocks on **Critical** and **High** only. Medium, Low, and Info pass through.

### Step File Conventions (from architecture + existing steps)

- Frontmatter: `nextStepFile`, `outputFile` (match existing steps)
- Section heading: `## STEP GOAL:` (TS convention — not `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE`)
- Update `stepsCompleted` in output file frontmatter before chaining
- Communicate in `{communication_language}`
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with default action and log each auto-decision
- Zero hallucination — every finding must trace to actual code with file:line citations

### Headless Envelope on Hard Gate Block

When the hard gate blocks in headless mode, emit to **stderr**:

```
SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","skill_name":"…","verdict":"FAIL","score":null,"threshold":null,"report_path":"…","next_workflow":"update-skill","exit_code":2,"halt_reason":"hard-gate-blocked"}
```

This follows the existing HALT envelope pattern from SKILL.md's Result Contract section. Note `score: null` because scoring was never reached. `exit_code: 2` per the exit code table (fail verdict). Add `"hard-gate-blocked"` to the `halt_reason` enum in SKILL.md's Result Contract documentation.

### SKILL.md Stages Table Update

Insert the hard gate as step 4c (between External Validators 4b and Score 5):

```
| 4c | Hard Gate | references/step-hard-gate.md | Yes |
```

Auto-proceed = Yes because the hard gate is deterministic (scan findings, block or pass — no user input needed).

### What Must NOT Change

- `score.md` — no modifications; the scoring step continues to work exactly as-is
- `coverage-check.md` — no modifications; findings continue to be written in the existing format
- `coherence-check.md` — no modifications; findings continue to be written in the existing format
- `report.md` — no modifications; gap report section is populated after scoring
- The gap entry format — hard gate reads the existing format, does not change it
- Exit codes 0, 3, 4 — these remain for score.md/report.md. The hard gate uses exit code 2 (fail) on block.

### Hard Gate Does NOT Score

The hard gate is a binary pass/block check. It does not compute a coverage percentage, does not use the `compute-score.py` script, and does not reference the `min:N` threshold. It only examines finding severity.

### Project Structure Notes

- New file path: `src/skf-test-skill/references/step-hard-gate.md` — matches the architecture FR→File mapping (FR-11)
- No new Python scripts needed — the hard gate is implemented entirely as a step file (LLM judgment on finding severity markers)
- Structural integration tests added in `test/test-skf-step-hard-gate.py` (35+ test cases covering pipeline chain, frontmatter, structure, severity scanning, block/pass paths, headless envelope, stages table, exit codes, result contract, and stepsCompleted integration)

### Epic 2 Retro Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After changing `external-validators.md` frontmatter, grep for any other files that reference `external-validators.md`'s `nextStepFile` or assume `score.md` follows `external-validators.md`
3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections within the step file (§1, §2, etc.) as established in existing TS step files
4. **Backward compatibility**: Verify the hard gate step chains correctly — score.md must still receive all the data it expects from prior steps

### Cross-Reference Check

After modifying `external-validators.md`, verify these do not hardcode the `external-validators → score` chain:
- `src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md` stages table (MUST update)
- `src/skf-test-skill/templates/test-report-template.md` anchor comments (MUST review)
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md` (references `stepsCompleted` — should pick up `'hard-gate'` naturally)

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md — Story 3.1 "Tiered quality gate — hard gate"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Quality Gate Architecture" section]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md — Stages table, Exit Codes, Result Contract]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/score.md — Step 5 scoring flow]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/scoring-rules.md — Gap Severity table]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/assets/output-section-formats.md — Gap Entry Format]
- [Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/external-validators.md — frontmatter nextStepFile: 'score.md']
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/epic-2-retro-2026-05-26.md — Commitments section]

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

### Debug Log References

Cross-reference grep revealed `report.md` hardcodes the stepsCompleted canonical chain at line 170-177. The story's Dev Notes said "should pick up 'hard-gate' naturally" — but the list is explicit, so `'hard-gate'` was added between `'external-validators'` and `'score'`. Without this fix, step 6 §5 would HALT with "step completeness violation — missing hard-gate".

### Completion Notes List

- Created `step-hard-gate.md` with §1-§4 sections following TS step file conventions (frontmatter, STEP GOAL heading, §-prefixed sections)
- Hard gate scans Coverage Analysis and Coherence Analysis for `**Severity:** Critical` and `**Severity:** High` markers
- Block path (§3): sets `testResult: 'fail'`, emits headless envelope to stderr, HALTs
- Pass path (§4): logs finding count, updates stepsCompleted, chains to score.md
- Updated external-validators.md frontmatter to chain to step-hard-gate.md
- Updated SKILL.md: stages table (4c Hard Gate row), exit code 2 raised-by (step 4c §3), halt_reason enum (hard-gate-blocked)
- Updated test-report-template.md anchor comment to note hard gate role (no new report section)
- Fixed report.md stepsCompleted canonical chain to include 'hard-gate'
- All tests pass (0 errors across schemas, install, CLI, workflow, Python, knowledge, validation, lint, format)

### Change Log

- 2026-05-26: Implemented hard gate step (Story 3.1) — binary pass/block check between external-validators and score that blocks on critical/high findings

### File List

- src/skf-test-skill/references/step-hard-gate.md (new)
- src/skf-test-skill/references/external-validators.md (modified — frontmatter nextStepFile)

5	# Story 3.2: Per-Pipeline Quality Thresholds
6	
7	Status: done
8	
9	## Story
10	
11	As a pipeline operator,
12	I want each pipeline alias to declare its own default quality threshold,
13	So that deepwiki skills are held to 90% while forge skills default to 80%.
14	
15	## Acceptance Criteria
16	
17	1. **Given** a TS invocation within the deepwiki pipeline and no explicit override
18	   **When** TS looks up the threshold
19	   **Then** the default threshold is 90%
20	
21	2. **Given** a TS invocation within the forge pipeline and no explicit override
22	   **When** TS looks up the threshold
23	   **Then** the default threshold is 80%
24	
25	3. **Given** a TS invocation with the `TS[min:85]` override syntax
26	   **When** TS resolves the threshold
27	   **Then** the override value of 85% is used instead of the pipeline default
28	   **And** the source of the threshold (override vs default) is logged
29	
30	4. **Given** any pipeline and any threshold configuration
31	   **When** TS runs
32	   **Then** the hard gate (Story 3.1) is enforced before the soft gate regardless of threshold value
33	
34	## Tasks / Subtasks
35	
36	- [x] Task 1: Add per-pipeline threshold lookup table to init.md (AC: #1, #2, #4)
37	  - [x] 1.1 Add a `pipeline_threshold_defaults` lookup table as a new §1b section after §1 and before §2
38	  - [x] 1.2 Resolve `{pipeline_alias}` from workflow data context (set by forger during pipeline execution)
39	  - [x] 1.3 If `{pipeline_alias}` is present AND found in the lookup table, store `{pipeline_default_threshold}` in workflow context
40	  - [x] 1.4 If `{pipeline_alias}` is absent (standalone TS invocation), `{pipeline_default_threshold}` is unset — score.md falls through to `{defaultThreshold}`
41

43	  - [x] 2.1 Update §1 "Resolve the pass threshold" to add pipeline default as a precedence layer
44	  - [x] 2.2 New precedence order: CLI `--threshold=N` > `{pipeline_default_threshold}` (from init.md §1b lookup) > `{defaultThreshold}` (from customize.toml, default 80) > fallback 80
45	  - [x] 2.3 Add threshold source logging: record which layer supplied `effective_threshold` (e.g. "pipeline default (deepwiki: 90)" or "CLI override (85)" or "workflow default (80)")
46	  - [x] 2.4 Store `threshold_source` string in workflow context for use in the score report section
47	
48	- [x] Task 3: Pass `{pipeline_alias}` from forger pipeline data context (AC: #1, #2)
49	  - [x] 3.1 In `src/skf-forger/SKILL.md` Pipeline Mode §4.c, add `{pipeline_alias}` to the data context passed to each workflow in the chain (set to the alias name: `deepwiki`, `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, `maintain`, or `null` for ad-hoc sequences)
50	  - [x] 3.2 In `src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md` Pipeline State section, document `pipeline_alias` as part of the pipeline data context forwarded to each workflow
51
