1	---
2	baseline_commit: ab25d834aed60cad70bead87fb0fc37286a46675
3	---
4	
5	# Story 3.5: Pre-Apply Workaround Registry
6	
7	Status: done
8	
9	## Story
10	
11	As a pipeline operator,
12	I want known workarounds to be automatically applied before each pipeline iteration,
13	So that previously-solved issues are not re-discovered and manually fixed each time.
14	
15	## Acceptance Criteria
16	
17	1. **Given** a `_known-workarounds.yaml` registry with a fingerprint matching content in the target directory
18	   **When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>` runs
19	   **Then** the matching fix is applied to the target content
20	   **And** the applied fix is logged to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/preapply-log.json`
21	
22	2. **Given** a registry with no fingerprints matching the target content
23	   **When** `skf-preapply.py` runs
24	   **Then** no fixes are applied and the log records zero matches
25	
26	3. **Given** both a shared seed registry and a project-local registry with a colliding fingerprint
27	   **When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path> --local-registry <path>` runs
28	   **Then** the project-local fix wins on collision
29	   **And** the shared seed is loaded first, project-local merged on top
30	
31	4. **Given** a new workaround discovered during a pipeline run
32	   **When** it is recorded
33	   **Then** it is appended to the project-local registry only, never to the shared seed
34	
35	5. **Given** a fingerprint entry with `regex: true`
36	   **When** matching is performed
37	   **Then** regex matching is used instead of the default substring matching
38	
39	## Tasks / Subtasks
40	
41	- [x] Task 1: Create the `_known-workarounds.yaml` shared seed registry (AC: #1, #3, #4)
42	  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` with `version: 1` header and `workarounds:` array
43	  - [x] 1.2 Populate with 25 GMC campaign fingerprint-to-fix pairs following the schema: `{fingerprint, fix, source, severity, description}` per entry
44	  - [x] 1.3 Include entries spanning severity levels (low, medium, high) with a mix of substring and regex fingerprints
45	  - [x] 1.4 Add `regex: true` flag on entries that require regex matching (default is substring)
46	
47	- [x] Task 2: Create `skf-preapply.py` shared script (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
48	  - [x] 2.1 Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py` with PEP 723 header (`requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = ["pyyaml"]`)
49	  - [x] 2.2 Implement argparse CLI: `--target-dir <path>` (required), `--registry <path>` (optional, defaults to `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml`), `--local-registry <path>` (optional)
50	  - [x] 2.3 Implement registry loading: load shared seed first, then project-local; merge on fingerprint key with local winning on collision
51	  - [x] 2.4 Implement fingerprint matching: iterate target directory files, for each workaround entry check content via substring match (default) or regex match (`regex: true`)
52	  - [x] 2.5 Implement fix application: when a fingerprint matches, apply the fix to the matching content
53	  - [x] 2.6 Implement JSON stdout output: `{applied: [{fingerprint, fix, file, severity}], skipped_count: N, registry_version: 1}`
54	  - [x] 2.7 Implement exit codes: `0` = success (applied ≥ 0 fixes without errors), `2` = error (invalid args, missing registry, YAML parse error)
55	  - [x] 2.8 Implement stderr error output: `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` on exit 2
56	  - [x] 2.9 Implement preapply-log.json writing: write to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/preapply-log.json` if `--log-dir` is provided (or derive from target-dir context)
57	
58	- [x] Task 3: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
59	  - [x] 3.1 Create `test/test-skf-preapply.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-detect-docs.py` (importlib dynamic loading + subprocess CLI tests)
60	  - [x] 3.2 Test: `_known-workarounds.yaml` exists at `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml`
61	  - [x] 3.3 Test: registry has `version: 1` header
62	  - [x] 3.4 Test: registry has `workarounds` array with ≥ 25 entries
63	  - [x] 3.5 Test: each entry has required fields: `fingerprint`, `fix`, `source`, `severity`, `description`
64	  - [x] 3.6 Test: `severity` values are in `{low, medium, high}`
65	  - [x] 3.7 Test: at least one entry has `regex: true`
66	  - [x] 3.8 Test: `skf-preapply.py` exists at expected path
67	  - [x] 3.9 Test: `skf-preapply.py` has PEP 723 header with `pyyaml` dependency
68	  - [x] 3.10 Test: CLI accepts `--target-dir`, `--registry`, `--local-registry` args
69	  - [x] 3.11 Test: JSON stdout schema matches `{applied, skipped_count, registry_version}`
70	  - [x] 3.12 Test: exit code 0 on valid invocation (with temp directory target)
71	  - [x] 3.13 Test: exit code 2 on missing target-dir or invalid registry
72	  - [x] 3.14 Test: substring matching applies fix to matching content
73	  - [x] 3.15 Test: regex matching applies fix when `regex: true`
74	  - [x] 3.16 Test: local registry overrides shared seed on fingerprint collision
75	  - [x] 3.17 Test: no modifications to target files when no fingerprints match
76	  - [x] 3.18 Register `test/test-skf-preapply.py` in `package.json` test:python command
77	
78	## Dev Notes
79	
80	### Script Interface (from Architecture)
81	
82	```
83	skf-preapply.py
84	Args: --target-dir <path> [--registry <path>] [--local-registry <path>]
85	Stdout: JSON {applied[], skipped_count, registry_version}
86	Exit: 0 (success), 2 (error)
87	```
88	
89	Note: exit code is `0` for both "applied fixes" and "zero matches" — both are successful runs. Exit `1` is NOT used by this script (unlike detect-docs where `1` = "none found"). The architecture specifies only exit `0` and `2` for preapply.
90	
91	### Registry Schema (from Architecture)
92	
93	```yaml
94	version: 1
95	workarounds:
96	  - fingerprint: string     # pattern to match in target content
97	    fix: string             # correction text or instruction
98	    source: string          # where the workaround was discovered
99	    severity: low|medium|high
100	    description: string     # one-line human summary
101	    regex: true             # optional — use regex matching instead of substring
102	```
103	
104	### Merge Strategy
105	
106	1. Load shared seed registry (`src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` or `--registry` path)
107	2. Load project-local registry (`--local-registry` path, if provided)
108	3. Merge: iterate project-local entries; on fingerprint collision (same `fingerprint` string), local entry replaces shared entry
109	4. Merged registry is ephemeral — never persisted as a combined file
110	
111	**Anti-pattern (from Architecture):** Modifying the shared seed file during a pipeline run. The shared seed is an SKF source file — only maintainer PRs change it.
112	
113	### Fingerprint Matching
114	
115	- Default: substring match — `fingerprint` string is searched within each target file's content
116	- Regex opt-in: when entry has `regex: true`, `fingerprint` is compiled as a Python `re` pattern
117	- Match target: all `.md` files within `--target-dir` (step files, SKILL.md, reference docs)
118	- Files are read with `encoding="utf-8"` (cross-platform safety per PR #311/#316/#366/#368/#387)
119	
120	### Fix Application
121	
122	When a fingerprint matches:
123	1. Apply the `fix` as a replacement for the matched content
124	2. Log the application to the `applied` array in stdout JSON
125	3. If `--log-dir` is provided, write detailed log to `preapply-log.json` at that path
126	
127	### Consumers of Pre-Apply
128	
129	- **All pipeline runs:** Pre-apply runs before each pipeline iteration (shared module)
130	- **Campaign step-05 (skill-loop):** Per-skill pre-apply → kickoff → AN → BS → CS → TS
131	- **Standalone invocation:** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>`
132	
133	### Shared Script Conventions (from Architecture + Stories 3.1–3.4)
134	
135	1. **PEP 723 header:** `requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = ["pyyaml"]`
136	2. **Module docstring:** describe CLI, input/output, exit codes
137	3. **argparse:** GNU-style long flags, kebab-case (`--target-dir`, not `--target_dir`)
138	4. **JSON stdout:** one object, no pretty-print in non-interactive mode
139	5. **Errors:** JSON `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` to stderr on exit 2
140	6. **No global state, no env var dependencies** beyond what uv provides
141	7. **Invocation:** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>`
142	8. **Paths:** Use `Path.as_posix()` in assertions, not `str()` — cross-platform safety
143	9. **File reads:** Always `encoding="utf-8"` — avoid cp1252 mojibake on Windows
144	10. **File writes:** Use `os.open` with `O_BINARY` flag for byte-exact writes if needed
145	
146	### What the GMC Seed Fingerprints Cover
147	
148	The 25 GMC campaign fingerprints capture recurring workarounds discovered during batch skill-forging campaigns. They address common issues like:
149	- Hallucinated API endpoints or function signatures in compiled skills
150	- Stale version references carried from outdated documentation
151	- Inconsistent heading structures that fail skill-check validation
152	- Missing provenance markers (T1 annotations, confidence tiers)
153	- Repeated citation format errors
154	- Common markdown lint violations in generated content
155	- Section ordering violations against the skill-sections spec
156	- Broken cross-reference links to non-existent reference files
157	
158	Each fingerprint is a stable textual pattern that recurs across multiple skill compilations. The fix is the deterministic correction.
159	
160	### Project Structure Notes
161	
162	New files:
163	- `src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py` — the pre-apply shared script
164	- `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` — shared seed registry (25 GMC fingerprints)
165	- `test/test-skf-preapply.py` — structural integration tests
166	
167	Modified files:
168	- `package.json` — add `test/test-skf-preapply.py` to `test:python` command
169	
170	No existing step files or workflows are modified — this story creates the standalone shared module. Integration into pipeline flows (calling pre-apply before each iteration) will happen at the pipeline level in downstream stories or campaign (Story 4.6).
171	
172	### Story 3.4 Learnings (Apply These)
173

# Story 3.5: Pre-Apply Workaround Registry

Status: done

## Story

As a pipeline operator,
I want known workarounds to be automatically applied before each pipeline iteration,
So that previously-solved issues are not re-discovered and manually fixed each time.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a `_known-workarounds.yaml` registry with a fingerprint matching content in the target directory
   **When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>` runs
   **Then** the matching fix is applied to the target content
   **And** the applied fix is logged to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/preapply-log.json`

2. **Given** a registry with no fingerprints matching the target content
   **When** `skf-preapply.py` runs
   **Then** no fixes are applied and the log records zero matches

3. **Given** both a shared seed registry and a project-local registry with a colliding fingerprint
   **When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path> --local-registry <path>` runs
   **Then** the project-local fix wins on collision
   **And** the shared seed is loaded first, project-local merged on top

4. **Given** a new workaround discovered during a pipeline run
   **When** it is recorded
   **Then** it is appended to the project-local registry only, never to the shared seed

5. **Given** a fingerprint entry with `regex: true`
   **When** matching is performed
   **Then** regex matching is used instead of the default substring matching

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create the `_known-workarounds.yaml` shared seed registry (AC: #1, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` with `version: 1` header and `workarounds:` array
  - [x] 1.2 Populate with 25 GMC campaign fingerprint-to-fix pairs following the schema: `{fingerprint, fix, source, severity, description}` per entry
  - [x] 1.3 Include entries spanning severity levels (low, medium, high) with a mix of substring and regex fingerprints
  - [x] 1.4 Add `regex: true` flag on entries that require regex matching (default is substring)

- [x] Task 2: Create `skf-preapply.py` shared script (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
  - [x] 2.1 Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py` with PEP 723 header (`requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = ["pyyaml"]`)
  - [x] 2.2 Implement argparse CLI: `--target-dir <path>` (required), `--registry <path>` (optional, defaults to `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml`), `--local-registry <path>` (optional)
  - [x] 2.3 Implement registry loading: load shared seed first, then project-local; merge on fingerprint key with local winning on collision
  - [x] 2.4 Implement fingerprint matching: iterate target directory files, for each workaround entry check content via substring match (default) or regex match (`regex: true`)
  - [x] 2.5 Implement fix application: when a fingerprint matches, apply the fix to the matching content
  - [x] 2.6 Implement JSON stdout output: `{applied: [{fingerprint, fix, file, severity}], skipped_count: N, registry_version: 1}`
  - [x] 2.7 Implement exit codes: `0` = success (applied ≥ 0 fixes without errors), `2` = error (invalid args, missing registry, YAML parse error)
  - [x] 2.8 Implement stderr error output: `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` on exit 2
  - [x] 2.9 Implement preapply-log.json writing: write to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/preapply-log.json` if `--log-dir` is provided (or derive from target-dir context)

- [x] Task 3: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
  - [x] 3.1 Create `test/test-skf-preapply.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-detect-docs.py` (importlib dynamic loading + subprocess CLI tests)
  - [x] 3.2 Test: `_known-workarounds.yaml` exists at `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml`
  - [x] 3.3 Test: registry has `version: 1` header
  - [x] 3.4 Test: registry has `workarounds` array with ≥ 25 entries
  - [x] 3.5 Test: each entry has required fields: `fingerprint`, `fix`, `source`, `severity`, `description`
  - [x] 3.6 Test: `severity` values are in `{low, medium, high}`
  - [x] 3.7 Test: at least one entry has `regex: true`
  - [x] 3.8 Test: `skf-preapply.py` exists at expected path
  - [x] 3.9 Test: `skf-preapply.py` has PEP 723 header with `pyyaml` dependency
  - [x] 3.10 Test: CLI accepts `--target-dir`, `--registry`, `--local-registry` args
  - [x] 3.11 Test: JSON stdout schema matches `{applied, skipped_count, registry_version}`
  - [x] 3.12 Test: exit code 0 on valid invocation (with temp directory target)
  - [x] 3.13 Test: exit code 2 on missing target-dir or invalid registry
  - [x] 3.14 Test: substring matching applies fix to matching content
  - [x] 3.15 Test: regex matching applies fix when `regex: true`
  - [x] 3.16 Test: local registry overrides shared seed on fingerprint collision
  - [x] 3.17 Test: no modifications to target files when no fingerprints match
  - [x] 3.18 Register `test/test-skf-preapply.py` in `package.json` test:python command

## Dev Notes

### Script Interface (from Architecture)

```
skf-preapply.py
Args: --target-dir <path> [--registry <path>] [--local-registry <path>]
Stdout: JSON {applied[], skipped_count, registry_version}
Exit: 0 (success), 2 (error)
```

Note: exit code is `0` for both "applied fixes" and "zero matches" — both are successful runs. Exit `1` is NOT used by this script (unlike detect-docs where `1` = "none found"). The architecture specifies only exit `0` and `2` for preapply.

### Registry Schema (from Architecture)

```yaml
version: 1
workarounds:
  - fingerprint: string     # pattern to match in target content
    fix: string             # correction text or instruction
    source: string          # where the workaround was discovered
    severity: low|medium|high
    description: string     # one-line human summary
    regex: true             # optional — use regex matching instead of substring
```

### Merge Strategy

1. Load shared seed registry (`src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` or `--registry` path)
2. Load project-local registry (`--local-registry` path, if provided)
3. Merge: iterate project-local entries; on fingerprint collision (same `fingerprint` string), local entry replaces shared entry
4. Merged registry is ephemeral — never persisted as a combined file

**Anti-pattern (from Architecture):** Modifying the shared seed file during a pipeline run. The shared seed is an SKF source file — only maintainer PRs change it.

### Fingerprint Matching

- Default: substring match — `fingerprint` string is searched within each target file's content
- Regex opt-in: when entry has `regex: true`, `fingerprint` is compiled as a Python `re` pattern
- Match target: all `.md` files within `--target-dir` (step files, SKILL.md, reference docs)
- Files are read with `encoding="utf-8"` (cross-platform safety per PR #311/#316/#366/#368/#387)

### Fix Application

When a fingerprint matches:
1. Apply the `fix` as a replacement for the matched content
2. Log the application to the `applied` array in stdout JSON
3. If `--log-dir` is provided, write detailed log to `preapply-log.json` at that path

### Consumers of Pre-Apply

- **All pipeline runs:** Pre-apply runs before each pipeline iteration (shared module)
- **Campaign step-05 (skill-loop):** Per-skill pre-apply → kickoff → AN → BS → CS → TS
- **Standalone invocation:** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>`

### Shared Script Conventions (from Architecture + Stories 3.1–3.4)

1. **PEP 723 header:** `requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = ["pyyaml"]`
2. **Module docstring:** describe CLI, input/output, exit codes
3. **argparse:** GNU-style long flags, kebab-case (`--target-dir`, not `--target_dir`)
4. **JSON stdout:** one object, no pretty-print in non-interactive mode
5. **Errors:** JSON `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` to stderr on exit 2
6. **No global state, no env var dependencies** beyond what uv provides
7. **Invocation:** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>`
8. **Paths:** Use `Path.as_posix()` in assertions, not `str()` — cross-platform safety
9. **File reads:** Always `encoding="utf-8"` — avoid cp1252 mojibake on Windows
10. **File writes:** Use `os.open` with `O_BINARY` flag for byte-exact writes if needed

### What the GMC Seed Fingerprints Cover

The 25 GMC campaign fingerprints capture recurring workarounds discovered during batch skill-forging campaigns. They address common issues like:
- Hallucinated API endpoints or function signatures in compiled skills
- Stale version references carried from outdated documentation
- Inconsistent heading structures that fail skill-check validation
- Missing provenance markers (T1 annotations, confidence tiers)
- Repeated citation format errors
- Common markdown lint violations in generated content
- Section ordering violations against the skill-sections spec
- Broken cross-reference links to non-existent reference files

Each fingerprint is a stable textual pattern that recurs across multiple skill compilations. The fix is the deterministic correction.

### Project Structure Notes

New files:
- `src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py` — the pre-apply shared script
- `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` — shared seed registry (25 GMC fingerprints)
- `test/test-skf-preapply.py` — structural integration tests

Modified files:
- `package.json` — add `test/test-skf-preapply.py` to `test:python` command

No existing step files or workflows are modified — this story creates the standalone shared module. Integration into pipeline flows (calling pre-apply before each iteration) will happen at the pipeline level in downstream stories or campaign (Story 4.6).

### Story 3.4 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Register test file in package.json:** The `test:python` command enumerates every test path — adding `test/test-skf-preapply.py` without updating it leaves the test dormant in CI
2. **Self-audit for internal references:** Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the script content, registry comments, or commit messages
3. **Cross-platform path assertions:** Use `Path.as_posix()` not `str()` in test assertions; read with `encoding="utf-8"`; windows-latest CI is the gatekeeper
4. **Structural test pattern:** Follow `test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py` for structural tests (pathlib-based, file existence, content regex matching) and `test-skf-detect-docs.py` for functional tests (importlib dynamic loading, subprocess CLI verification)

### Story 3.1 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Report.md stepsCompleted chain:** Not applicable to this story — pre-apply is a shared script, not a step in a workflow chain
2. **Stages table alignment:** Not applicable — no new step file in any workflow's Stages table

### What Must NOT Change

- Existing shared scripts — pre-apply is a new script, no modifications to existing ones
- The shared seed registry (`src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml`) must never be modified at runtime
- `forge-data/` structure — pre-apply writes logs there but does not modify the directory layout

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md — Story 3.5 "Pre-apply workaround registry"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Shared Module Interfaces" section: skf-preapply.py interface]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Pre-Apply Registry Patterns" section: merge strategy, fingerprint matching, fix logging]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Project Structure & Boundaries" section: src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml, forge-data/ runtime artifacts]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Shared Script Patterns" section: naming, args, output, exit codes]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Seed ↔ Project-local boundary" section: read-only seed, append-only local, ephemeral merge]
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-4-auto-shard-at-400-line-ceiling.md — Story 3.4 learnings: package.json registration, cross-platform paths]

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

### Debug Log References

### Completion Notes List

- Created shared seed registry with 25 GMC campaign fingerprint-to-fix pairs spanning low/medium/high severity, including 4 regex-enabled entries
- Implemented skf-preapply.py with PEP 723 header, argparse CLI (--target-dir, --registry, --local-registry, --log-dir), registry merge logic (local wins on collision), substring + regex fingerprint matching, JSON stdout output, structured stderr errors, and optional preapply-log.json writing
- Created 39 tests covering registry structure, script PEP 723 compliance, CLI args, output schema, exit codes (0 and 2), substring matching, regex matching, local-override merge, no-match passthrough, log-dir writing, nested dir scanning, multi-file matching, and invalid regex handling
- Registered test/test-skf-preapply.py in package.json test:python command

### Change Log

- 2026-05-27: Implemented pre-apply workaround registry — shared seed with 25 fingerprints, skf-preapply.py script, and 39 structural/functional tests
- 2026-05-27: Review fixes — added NoReturn annotation to _err(), added re.error handling for invalid regex fingerprints, removed dead importlib import from tests, added invalid regex test

### File List

- src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml (new)
- src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py (new)
- test/test-skf-preapply.py (new)
- package.json (modified — added test/test-skf-preapply.py to test:python)

4	
5	# Story 3.6: Doc-Rot Correction Hooks
6	
7	Status: done
8	
9	## Story
10	
11	As a skill author,
12	I want corrected items in feeder artifacts to be automatically annotated post-compile,
13	So that doc-rot corrections are surfaced deterministically without relying on AI judgment.
14	
15	## Acceptance Criteria
16	
17	1. **Given** a feeder artifact containing corrected content (identifiable via grep patterns)
18	   **When** the doc-rot hook fires post-compile
19	   **Then** correction blocks (`## CORRECTION`) are annotated in the compiled output
20	
21	2. **Given** the doc-rot hook
22	   **When** it performs matching
23	   **Then** matching is grep-based and deterministic (no AI judgment involved)
24	
25	3. **Given** a standalone deepwiki pipeline run (not a campaign)
26	   **When** CS completes compilation
27	   **Then** the doc-rot hook fires (it is not campaign-only)
28	
29	4. **Given** a feeder artifact with no corrected content
30	   **When** the doc-rot hook fires
31	   **Then** no correction blocks are added and the output is unchanged
32	
33	## Tasks / Subtasks
34	
35	- [x] Task 1: Create `step-doc-rot.md` step file (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
36	  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md` with frontmatter `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`
37	  - [x] 1.2 Define the step goal: scan feeder artifacts for doc-rot correction indicators and annotate compiled output with `## CORRECTION` blocks
38	  - [x] 1.3 Define the Rules section: auto-proceed, no user interaction, graceful skip when no corrections found, do not modify anything except the compiled SKILL.md and references
39	  - [x] 1.4 Define the mandatory grep pattern list for correction detection (see Dev Notes — Grep Patterns)
40	  - [x] 1.5 Define the feeder artifact scan targets: evidence-report.md, provenance-map.json (T2/T3 entries with temporal annotations), temporal docs in context from step 3b/4
41	  - [x] 1.6 Define the `## CORRECTION` block format: heading, source citation, pattern matched, affected API/section
42	  - [x] 1.7 Define the insertion logic: correction blocks appended after the relevant section in SKILL.md (or at end if section cannot be determined)
43	  - [x] 1.8 Define the graceful skip path: no feeder artifacts with corrections → log skip message → proceed to validate
44	  - [x] 1.9 Define the auto-proceed to `{nextStepFile}` at end of step
45	
46	- [x] Task 2: Update step chain to include doc-rot hook (AC: #3)
47	  - [x] 2.1 Modify `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` frontmatter: change `nextStepFile` from `'validate.md'` to `'step-doc-rot.md'`
48	  - [x] 2.2 Update `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` Stages table: add row for step 5c (Doc-Rot) between 5b (Auto-Shard) and 6 (Validate)
49	
50	- [x] Task 3: Create structural integration test (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
51	  - [x] 3.1 Create `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` (pathlib-based structural tests)
52	  - [x] 3.2 Test: `step-doc-rot.md` exists at `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md`
53	  - [x] 3.3 Test: step file has frontmatter with `nextStepFile` field
54	  - [x] 3.4 Test: `nextStepFile` value is `'validate.md'`
55	  - [x] 3.5 Test: step file contains the mandatory grep pattern list (at least 5 defined patterns)
56	  - [x] 3.6 Test: step file defines the `## CORRECTION` block format
57	  - [x] 3.7 Test: step file references feeder artifact scan targets (evidence-report, provenance-map, temporal)
58	  - [x] 3.8 Test: step file includes graceful skip logic when no corrections are found
59	  - [x] 3.9 Test: step-auto-shard.md chains to step-doc-rot.md (nextStepFile updated)
60	  - [x] 3.10 Test: CS SKILL.md Stages table includes Doc-Rot step entry
61	  - [x] 3.11 Register `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` in `package.json` test:python command
62	
63	## Dev Notes
64	
65	### Where This Step Fits in the CS Pipeline
66	
67	The current CS step chain (post-compile):
68	```
69	5. Compile → 5a. Doc Sources → 5b. Auto-Shard → 6. Validate → 7. Generate Artifacts → 8. Report → 9. Health Check
70	```
71	
72	After this story:
73	```
74	5. Compile → 5a. Doc Sources → 5b. Auto-Shard → 5c. Doc-Rot → 6. Validate → 7. Generate Artifacts → 8. Report → 9. Health Check
75	```
76	
77	The chain update requires TWO changes:
78	1. `step-auto-shard.md` frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'step-doc-rot.md'` (currently `'validate.md'`)
79	2. `step-doc-rot.md` frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`
80	
81	### Feeder Artifact Scan Targets
82	
83	"Feeder artifacts" in the CS workflow are the intermediate artifacts produced during extraction and enrichment that fed the compilation:
84	
85	1. **Evidence report** (`forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/evidence-report.md`) — contains compile warnings, signature conflicts, and enrichment notes that may reference corrections
86	2. **Provenance map** (`forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/provenance-map.json`) — T2/T3 entries with temporal annotations (deprecation markers, breaking change citations from QMD enrichment)
87	3. **Temporal context in workflow context** — changelogs, migration guides, and issue/PR data fetched by step 3b and enriched by step 4. If a temporal source noted a breaking change or deprecation for a documented API, that's a correction signal
88	4. **The compiled SKILL.md itself** — may contain T2 `[QMD:...]` annotations or T3 `[DOC:...]` citations that reference corrections in their source material
89	
90	### Grep Patterns for Correction Detection
91	
92	The step file MUST define an explicit, enumerated list of grep patterns. The LLM executing the step uses these patterns mechanically — no open-ended "assess whether content seems corrected." Minimum required patterns:
93	
94	| Pattern | Category | Target |
95	|---------|----------|--------|
96	| `deprecated` / `@deprecated` | Deprecation | API entries marked deprecated in temporal docs or source |
97	| `breaking change` / `BREAKING` | Breaking change | Changelog/migration guide entries |
98	| `removed in` / `was removed` | Removal | Functions/params removed between versions |
99	| `renamed to` / `renamed from` | Rename | API renames noted in changelogs |
100	| `superseded by` / `replaced by` | Supersession | APIs replaced by newer alternatives |
101	| `no longer supported` | End of life | Features/APIs dropped from support |
102	| `migration required` | Migration | Mandatory migration steps for consumers |
103	| `signature changed` | Signature change | Provenance map entries with T1/T2 signature conflicts |
104	
105	These patterns are scanned as case-insensitive substring matches against feeder artifact content. The step file defines them as a literal list, not as configurable input.
106	
107	### `## CORRECTION` Block Format
108	
109	When a correction is detected, the step annotates the compiled SKILL.md with a correction block:
110	
111	```markdown
112	## CORRECTION
113	
114	**Source:** {feeder_artifact_path_or_citation}
115	**Pattern:** {matched_pattern}
116	**Affected:** {function_name_or_section}
117	**Detail:** {one-line excerpt from the feeder artifact showing the correction context}
118	```
119	
120	Correction blocks are inserted:
121	- **After the relevant API section** in SKILL.md if the affected function/section can be identified
122	- **At the end of SKILL.md body** (before any trailing sections) if the affected section cannot be determined
123	- **Never inside frontmatter** — body content only
124	
125	Multiple corrections produce multiple `## CORRECTION` blocks. Each block is self-contained with its source citation.
126	
127	### Architecture Compliance
128	
129	From `architecture.md`:
130	- **FR-19:** "Post-compile hook in CS greps feeder artifacts for corrected items, annotates with correction blocks"
131	- **Step file pattern:** frontmatter with `nextStepFile`, RULES section, MANDATORY SEQUENCE, auto-proceed at end
132	- **Anti-pattern (from Architecture):** "A step that stores intermediate results in conversation context instead of writing to disk." — If corrections are found, write them to the staged SKILL.md on disk
133	- **Scope:** This step runs in ALL CS invocations (standalone forge, deepwiki, campaign per-skill loop) — it is NOT campaign-only
134	
135	### Campaign Integration (Future — NOT This Story)
136	
137	Campaign step-05 (skill-loop) references doc-rot as part of the per-skill sequence: `pre-apply → kickoff → AN → BS → CS → TS → mark complete → propagate findings → doc-rot check`. This story makes doc-rot fire INSIDE CS (as step 5c), so campaign gets it automatically when it invokes CS. No separate campaign integration is needed.
138	
139	### What Must Change (UPDATE Files)
140	
141	1. `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` — frontmatter `nextStepFile` update from `'validate.md'` to `'step-doc-rot.md'`
142	2. `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` — Stages table: add step 5c row
143	
144	Both files have been read in this story creation session. The step-auto-shard.md currently has:
145	```yaml
146	---
147	nextStepFile: 'validate.md'
148	---
149	```
150	This must change to:
151	```yaml
152	---

# Story 3.6: Doc-Rot Correction Hooks

Status: done

## Story

As a skill author,
I want corrected items in feeder artifacts to be automatically annotated post-compile,
So that doc-rot corrections are surfaced deterministically without relying on AI judgment.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a feeder artifact containing corrected content (identifiable via grep patterns)
   **When** the doc-rot hook fires post-compile
   **Then** correction blocks (`## CORRECTION`) are annotated in the compiled output

2. **Given** the doc-rot hook
   **When** it performs matching
   **Then** matching is grep-based and deterministic (no AI judgment involved)

3. **Given** a standalone deepwiki pipeline run (not a campaign)
   **When** CS completes compilation
   **Then** the doc-rot hook fires (it is not campaign-only)

4. **Given** a feeder artifact with no corrected content
   **When** the doc-rot hook fires
   **Then** no correction blocks are added and the output is unchanged

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create `step-doc-rot.md` step file (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md` with frontmatter `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`
  - [x] 1.2 Define the step goal: scan feeder artifacts for doc-rot correction indicators and annotate compiled output with `## CORRECTION` blocks
  - [x] 1.3 Define the Rules section: auto-proceed, no user interaction, graceful skip when no corrections found, do not modify anything except the compiled SKILL.md and references
  - [x] 1.4 Define the mandatory grep pattern list for correction detection (see Dev Notes — Grep Patterns)
  - [x] 1.5 Define the feeder artifact scan targets: evidence-report.md, provenance-map.json (T2/T3 entries with temporal annotations), temporal docs in context from step 3b/4
  - [x] 1.6 Define the `## CORRECTION` block format: heading, source citation, pattern matched, affected API/section
  - [x] 1.7 Define the insertion logic: correction blocks appended after the relevant section in SKILL.md (or at end if section cannot be determined)
  - [x] 1.8 Define the graceful skip path: no feeder artifacts with corrections → log skip message → proceed to validate
  - [x] 1.9 Define the auto-proceed to `{nextStepFile}` at end of step

- [x] Task 2: Update step chain to include doc-rot hook (AC: #3)
  - [x] 2.1 Modify `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` frontmatter: change `nextStepFile` from `'validate.md'` to `'step-doc-rot.md'`
  - [x] 2.2 Update `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` Stages table: add row for step 5c (Doc-Rot) between 5b (Auto-Shard) and 6 (Validate)

- [x] Task 3: Create structural integration test (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 3.1 Create `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` (pathlib-based structural tests)
  - [x] 3.2 Test: `step-doc-rot.md` exists at `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md`
  - [x] 3.3 Test: step file has frontmatter with `nextStepFile` field
  - [x] 3.4 Test: `nextStepFile` value is `'validate.md'`
  - [x] 3.5 Test: step file contains the mandatory grep pattern list (at least 5 defined patterns)
  - [x] 3.6 Test: step file defines the `## CORRECTION` block format
  - [x] 3.7 Test: step file references feeder artifact scan targets (evidence-report, provenance-map, temporal)
  - [x] 3.8 Test: step file includes graceful skip logic when no corrections are found
  - [x] 3.9 Test: step-auto-shard.md chains to step-doc-rot.md (nextStepFile updated)
  - [x] 3.10 Test: CS SKILL.md Stages table includes Doc-Rot step entry
  - [x] 3.11 Register `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` in `package.json` test:python command

## Dev Notes

### Where This Step Fits in the CS Pipeline

The current CS step chain (post-compile):
```
5. Compile → 5a. Doc Sources → 5b. Auto-Shard → 6. Validate → 7. Generate Artifacts → 8. Report → 9. Health Check
```

After this story:
```
5. Compile → 5a. Doc Sources → 5b. Auto-Shard → 5c. Doc-Rot → 6. Validate → 7. Generate Artifacts → 8. Report → 9. Health Check
```

The chain update requires TWO changes:
1. `step-auto-shard.md` frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'step-doc-rot.md'` (currently `'validate.md'`)
2. `step-doc-rot.md` frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`

### Feeder Artifact Scan Targets

"Feeder artifacts" in the CS workflow are the intermediate artifacts produced during extraction and enrichment that fed the compilation:

1. **Evidence report** (`forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/evidence-report.md`) — contains compile warnings, signature conflicts, and enrichment notes that may reference corrections
2. **Provenance map** (`forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/provenance-map.json`) — T2/T3 entries with temporal annotations (deprecation markers, breaking change citations from QMD enrichment)
3. **Temporal context in workflow context** — changelogs, migration guides, and issue/PR data fetched by step 3b and enriched by step 4. If a temporal source noted a breaking change or deprecation for a documented API, that's a correction signal
4. **The compiled SKILL.md itself** — may contain T2 `[QMD:...]` annotations or T3 `[DOC:...]` citations that reference corrections in their source material

### Grep Patterns for Correction Detection

The step file MUST define an explicit, enumerated list of grep patterns. The LLM executing the step uses these patterns mechanically — no open-ended "assess whether content seems corrected." Minimum required patterns:

| Pattern | Category | Target |
|---------|----------|--------|
| `deprecated` / `@deprecated` | Deprecation | API entries marked deprecated in temporal docs or source |
| `breaking change` / `BREAKING` | Breaking change | Changelog/migration guide entries |
| `removed in` / `was removed` | Removal | Functions/params removed between versions |
| `renamed to` / `renamed from` | Rename | API renames noted in changelogs |
| `superseded by` / `replaced by` | Supersession | APIs replaced by newer alternatives |
| `no longer supported` | End of life | Features/APIs dropped from support |
| `migration required` | Migration | Mandatory migration steps for consumers |
| `signature changed` | Signature change | Provenance map entries with T1/T2 signature conflicts |

These patterns are scanned as case-insensitive substring matches against feeder artifact content. The step file defines them as a literal list, not as configurable input.

### `## CORRECTION` Block Format

When a correction is detected, the step annotates the compiled SKILL.md with a correction block:

```markdown
## CORRECTION

**Source:** {feeder_artifact_path_or_citation}
**Pattern:** {matched_pattern}
**Affected:** {function_name_or_section}
**Detail:** {one-line excerpt from the feeder artifact showing the correction context}
```

Correction blocks are inserted:
- **After the relevant API section** in SKILL.md if the affected function/section can be identified
- **At the end of SKILL.md body** (before any trailing sections) if the affected section cannot be determined
- **Never inside frontmatter** — body content only

Multiple corrections produce multiple `## CORRECTION` blocks. Each block is self-contained with its source citation.

### Architecture Compliance

From `architecture.md`:
- **FR-19:** "Post-compile hook in CS greps feeder artifacts for corrected items, annotates with correction blocks"
- **Step file pattern:** frontmatter with `nextStepFile`, RULES section, MANDATORY SEQUENCE, auto-proceed at end
- **Anti-pattern (from Architecture):** "A step that stores intermediate results in conversation context instead of writing to disk." — If corrections are found, write them to the staged SKILL.md on disk
- **Scope:** This step runs in ALL CS invocations (standalone forge, deepwiki, campaign per-skill loop) — it is NOT campaign-only

### Campaign Integration (Future — NOT This Story)

Campaign step-05 (skill-loop) references doc-rot as part of the per-skill sequence: `pre-apply → kickoff → AN → BS → CS → TS → mark complete → propagate findings → doc-rot check`. This story makes doc-rot fire INSIDE CS (as step 5c), so campaign gets it automatically when it invokes CS. No separate campaign integration is needed.

### What Must Change (UPDATE Files)

1. `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` — frontmatter `nextStepFile` update from `'validate.md'` to `'step-doc-rot.md'`
2. `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` — Stages table: add step 5c row

Both files have been read in this story creation session. The step-auto-shard.md currently has:
```yaml
---
nextStepFile: 'validate.md'
---
```
This must change to:
```yaml
---
nextStepFile: 'step-doc-rot.md'
---
```

The SKILL.md Stages table currently has step 5b (Auto-Shard) immediately before step 6 (Validate). A new row must be inserted:
```
| 5c | Doc-Rot | references/step-doc-rot.md | Yes |
```

### What Must NOT Change

- No existing step files other than `step-auto-shard.md` (frontmatter only)
- No existing Python scripts — this is a step-file-only story
- No modifications to the pre-apply registry or workaround system
- No modifications to metadata.json, provenance-map.json, or evidence-report.md — this step READS them, does not write to them (it writes correction blocks only to SKILL.md)
- The validate step (`validate.md`) is unchanged — it continues to receive the next-step chain from doc-rot

### Project Structure Notes

New files:
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md` — the doc-rot correction hook step file
- `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` — structural integration tests

Modified files:
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` — frontmatter `nextStepFile` update
- `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` — Stages table update
- `package.json` — add `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` to `test:python` command

### Story 3.5 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Register test file in package.json:** The `test:python` command enumerates every test path — adding `test/test-skf-step-doc-rot.py` without updating it leaves the test dormant in CI
2. **Self-audit for internal references:** Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content, test file content, or commit messages
3. **Cross-platform path assertions:** Use `Path.as_posix()` not `str()` in test assertions; read with `encoding="utf-8"`
4. **Structural test pattern:** Follow `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` for structural tests (pathlib-based, file existence, content regex matching)

### Story 3.4 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Step file chain integrity:** When inserting a new step into the chain, verify BOTH the incoming chain (step-auto-shard → step-doc-rot) AND the outgoing chain (step-doc-rot → validate) are correct
2. **Stages table alignment:** When adding a step to the Stages table, verify the step number, file path, and auto-proceed column are all consistent

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md — Story 3.6 "Doc-rot correction hooks"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Modified Workflows" section: step-doc-rot.md listed as NEW in skf-create-skill/references/]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — FR-19 mapping: "Doc-rot hooks → src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Campaign Orchestration" section: step-05-skill-loop per-skill sequence includes doc-rot check]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Step File Patterns" section: frontmatter nextStepFile, stateless interpreters, write state before chaining]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md — current Stages table: 5b Auto-Shard → 6 Validate]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md — current nextStepFile: 'validate.md']
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md — pattern reference for auto-proceed step with graceful skip]
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-5-pre-apply-workaround-registry.md — learnings: package.json registration, cross-platform paths, structural test patterns]

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

### Debug Log References

- Auto-shard test regression detected and fixed: `test_next_step_file_is_validate` renamed to `test_next_step_file_is_doc_rot` to reflect new chain target

### Completion Notes List

- Created step-doc-rot.md (step 5c) with 5 §-prefixed sections, 13 grep patterns across 7 categories, CORRECTION block format, feeder artifact scan targets, graceful skip logic, and auto-proceed chaining
- Updated step-auto-shard.md frontmatter: nextStepFile changed from validate.md to step-doc-rot.md
- Inserted 5c Doc-Rot row in CS SKILL.md Stages table between 5b (Auto-Shard) and 6 (Validate)
- Created 47-test structural integration suite covering file existence, frontmatter, grep patterns, CORRECTION block format, feeder artifact targets, graceful skip, chain integrity, Stages table, structural contract, §-sections, context variables, auto-proceed, chain resolution, case-insensitive matching, and no-frontmatter-modification rule
- Updated auto-shard test to reflect new chain target (step-doc-rot.md instead of validate.md)
- Registered test file in package.json test:python command
- Full suite: 2050 passed, 0 failed

### Change Log

- 2026-05-27: Implemented doc-rot correction hooks — new CS step 5c between Auto-Shard and Validate, with deterministic grep-based correction detection and CORRECTION block annotation
- 2026-05-27: Code review — fixed copy-paste class name (TestAutoShardRules → TestDocRotRules), typo (TestAutoProceeed → TestAutoProceed), stale chain target message, added direct chain target existence test

- src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md (new)
- src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md (modified — frontmatter nextStepFile)
- src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md (modified — Stages table)

1	# Epic 2 Retrospective — deepwiki: Zero-Ceremony Skill Creation
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-05-26
4	**Facilitator:** bmad-retrospective (Amelia)
5	**Project Lead:** Armel
6	**Epic status at retro:** all 9 stories `done`; epic `in-progress` (to be flipped `done`); retrospective key `epic-2-retrospective` flipped `optional` → `done`.
7	**Previous retrospective:** epic-1-retro-2026-05-26.md (Source Intelligence Foundation — same day, earlier session).
8	
9	## Summary
10	
11	Epic 2 delivered the complete deepwiki zero-ceremony skill creation pipeline: 9 stories implementing auto-scope (AN), auto-brief with validation (BS), auto-decomposition, docs-only path, coexistence detection, version pinning, the `deepwiki` pipeline alias, `onboard` deprecation, and v1.9 user documentation. All 9 stories shipped on **2026-05-26/27** on the `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` branch.
12	
13	| Story | Outcome |
14	|-------|---------|
15	| 2.1 Auto-scope mode for AN | `step-auto-scope.md` + `step-shape-detect.md` created. `[auto]` routing in init.md. Review fixed 1 HIGH (go.mod references unsupported by shape-detect), 1 MEDIUM. |
16	| 2.2 Auto-brief generation for BS | `step-auto-brief.md` created. Doc detection enrichment with graceful degradation. Review fixed envelope emitter to support `mode` field (schema + script + tests). |
17	| 2.3 Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback | `step-auto-validate.md` created. Approve/Edit/Reject gate with headless auto-approve. Review fixed 4 issues: restored error envelope (critical), stale cross-ref, [E]dit path ordering, [R]eject field preservation. |
18	| 2.4 Auto-decomposition for massive repos | §3a/§4a/§5a/§6a conditional branch in step-auto-scope.md. Two thresholds (500 exports, 3 packages) with `[PENDING VALIDATION]` markers. Review fixed 3 issues (stale mapping note, missing validation markers, absent merge threshold). |
19	| 2.5 Docs-only deepwiki path | §0/§0a URL type detection and docs-only short-circuit. Conditional skip in step-auto-brief.md. Review fixed exit code alignment (2→3) and halt_reason alignment. |
20	| 2.6 Coexistence detection | §0c coexistence gate with A/M/S actions. Inventory-based matching. `-wiki` suffix for alongside. Review fixed 3 MEDIUM (envelope field, multi-match disambiguation, URL normalization). |
21	| 2.7 Version pinning for deepwiki | `skf-validate-pins.py` created (new shared script, 32 tests). §0b pin resolution gate. Review fixed 3 MEDIUM (redundant API call, no-op CLI test, internal story ID references). |
22	| 2.8 deepwiki pipeline alias + onboard deprecation | `deepwiki` alias registered in forger + pipeline-contracts.md. `[auto]` bracket modifier documented. `onboard` deprecation notice. Review fixed 2 issues (missing AN→BS data flow entry, internal story ref). |
23	| 2.9 v1.9 documentation | `docs/deepwiki.md` created. `docs/workflows.md` and `docs/bmad-synergy.md` updated. Review added deepwiki sidebar entry to astro config. |
24	
25	Total new test files: 1 (`test-skf-validate-pins.py` with 32 tests). Total test count: 1766 (from 1765).
26	
27	## What went well
28	
29	1. **Progressive story layering.** Stories built cleanly on each other: 2.1 (auto-scope) → 2.2 (auto-brief) → 2.3 (validation gate) → 2.4 (decomposition) → 2.5 (docs-only) → 2.6 (coexistence) → 2.7 (pinning) → 2.8 (alias registration) → 2.9 (documentation). Each story added a conditional branch or gate to step-auto-scope.md without disrupting previous work. The section numbering scheme (§0, §0a, §0b, §0c, §1, ...) accommodated insertions elegantly.
30	
31	2. **Consistent `[auto]` routing pattern across AN and BS.** Story 2.1 established the pattern (§2b auto check in init.md, route to step-auto-scope.md), and Story 2.2 replicated it identically (§1b auto check in gather-intent.md, route to step-auto-brief.md). Both used pure conditional branches with zero changes to interactive paths. This pattern is now a proven, repeatable convention for future auto-mode extensions.
32	
33	3. **Envelope extension discipline.** No new envelope types were created across all 9 stories. Every new field (`mode`, `source_type`, `pinned_ref`, `pinned_version`, `coexistence`) was added to existing `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` and `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelopes. Backward compatibility was maintained throughout — existing consumers ignore unknown fields.
34	
35	4. **Story intelligence carried forward effectively.** Every story's dev notes explicitly referenced patterns and learnings from prior stories (and the Epic 1 retro). The "Previous Story Intelligence" section in each story file became a genuine knowledge transfer mechanism. Story 2.7's review caught a no-op CLI test — the same class of bug the Epic 1 retro flagged in Story 1.2. The retro warning was heeded and the pattern was detected.
36	
37	5. **Documentation as a first-class deliverable.** Story 2.9 was a dedicated documentation story, not an afterthought. It produced `docs/deepwiki.md` as a focused user guide, updated `docs/workflows.md` with alias table and examples, and added a standalone-user section to `docs/bmad-synergy.md`. The review also caught the missing sidebar entry in `astro.config.mjs`.
38	
39	6. **Graceful degradation consistently applied.** Every auto feature has a defined fallback: unknown shape → interactive AN, doc detection failure → brief without docs, brief rejection → interactive BS, URL unreachable → clear error, no releases → HEAD, no existing skills → proceed silently. This pattern was established in the architecture and consistently implemented.
40	
41	7. **Code review discipline from Epic 1 sustained.** Every story went through structured review. Total across the epic: ~19 findings patched (2 CRITICAL, 8 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 4 informational). No unpatched CRITICAL or HIGH findings at close.
42	
43	## Patterns across all nine stories
44	
45	### Review consistently finds real issues
46	
47	| Story | Patches | Types of Findings |
48	|-------|---------|-------------------|
49	| 2.1 | 2 | Unsupported manifest type (HIGH), excess references |
50	| 2.2 | 2 | Envelope schema/script update, doc detection clarification |
51	| 2.3 | 4 | Dangling refs (CRITICAL), stale cross-ref, path ordering, field preservation |
52	| 2.4 | 3 | Stale mapping note, missing validation markers, absent threshold |
53	| 2.5 | 2 | Exit code misalignment, halt_reason misalignment |
54	| 2.6 | 3 | Missing envelope field, multi-match gap, URL normalization |
55	| 2.7 | 3 | Redundant API call, no-op test, internal story ref |
56	| 2.8 | 2 | Missing data flow entry, internal story ref |
57	| 2.9 | 1 | Missing sidebar entry |
58	
59	The "internal story reference in shipped source" finding appeared in both 2.7 and 2.8 — indicating a systematic habit that reviews consistently caught. The "no-op test" finding in 2.7 (same class as Epic 1's Story 1.2) confirms that mock-based testing remains a risk area.
60	
61	### Step-file section numbering accommodates progressive insertion
62	
63	step-auto-scope.md grew from 11 sections (§1-§11) in Story 2.1 to a flow with pre-sections (§0, §0a, §0b, §0c) and branching sections (§3a, §4a, §5a, §6a). The numbering convention (§0 before §1, §3a as a branch from §3) kept the document readable and allowed each story to insert without renumbering. This should be documented as a recommended pattern for future step file evolution.
64	
65	### Backward compatibility verified systematically in every story
66	
67	Every story explicitly checked that interactive paths, existing pipeline aliases (forge, forge-quick, onboard, maintain), and headless-without-auto mode were unaffected. The `[auto]` flag is the single entry point for all new behavior — nothing fires without it. Zero regressions across the full test suite.
68	
69	### One new shared script (vs. 9 new step files)
70	
71	Epic 2 created only one new Python script (`skf-validate-pins.py`), versus 4 new step files and modifications to 2 existing step files. This reflects the architectural principle that heuristic analysis (shape-to-scope mapping, decomposition boundaries, URL type detection) belongs in step files (LLM judgment), while deterministic computation (tag validation, semver parsing) goes in Python scripts.
72	
73	## Challenges and growth areas
74	
75	1. **Internal reference leakage into shipped source.** Stories 2.7 and 2.8 both shipped with internal story ID references ("Story 2.7", "story 2.7") in source files. Both were caught in review, but the pattern indicates a systematic tendency. The dev agent should be trained to self-audit for internal references before review.
76	
77	2. **Envelope schema evolution complexity.** While no new envelope types were created (good), the growing number of optional fields on existing envelopes (`mode`, `source_type`, `pinned_ref`, `pinned_version`, `coexistence`, `redirect_to`, `skipped_reason`) makes the envelope contract harder to reason about. No formal schema validation exists for these envelopes at runtime — correctness relies on step file discipline.
78	
79	3. **step-auto-scope.md accumulated significant complexity.** By Story 2.7, step-auto-scope.md contains: §0 (URL detection), §0b (pin resolution), §0c (coexistence detection), §0a (docs-only short-circuit), §1-§3 (standard flow), §3a (decomposition threshold), §4-§6 (single scope), §4a-§6a (multi-scope), §7-§11 (report/brief/envelope). This is the most complex step file in SKF. While each addition was clean in isolation, the aggregate complexity warrants monitoring.
80	
81	4. **No empirical validation of the complete deepwiki flow.** All stories were validated with `npm test` (structural tests), but the end-to-end deepwiki pipeline (`deepwiki <repo-url>`) has not been run against a real repository. The auto-scope heuristics, decomposition thresholds, and pin resolution all carry `[PENDING VALIDATION]` markers. The first real-world run will be the real test.
82	
83	5. **Story 2.3 review found a CRITICAL issue (dangling §7 references).** When step-auto-brief.md was modified to remove envelope emission, 4 downstream references to §7 became dangling. This was caught in review, but it highlights the risk of modifying step files that other step files cross-reference. Step file refactoring needs to include a cross-reference grep.
84	
85	## Technical debt carried forward
86	
87	| Item | Source | Severity | Impact on Epic 3 |
88	|------|--------|----------|-------------------|
89	| `[PENDING VALIDATION]` on 500-export threshold | Story 2.4 | Medium | None (decomposition is deepwiki-only; Epic 3 doesn't touch it) |
90	| `[PENDING VALIDATION]` on 3-package threshold | Story 2.4 | Medium | None |
91	| `[PENDING VALIDATION]` on ~50-export merge threshold | Story 2.4 | Low | None |
92	| Batch mode gap for deepwiki pipeline | Story 2.8 review | Low | None (single-repo deepwiki doesn't use batch) |
93	| Missing `@scope/name@version` tag matching | Story 2.7 review | Low | None (scoped tags need upstream enrichment) |
94	| step-auto-scope.md aggregate complexity | Cumulative | Medium | Indirect — Epic 3's step files should avoid this pattern |
95	| Envelope optional field proliferation | Cumulative | Low | Epic 3 adds quality gate fields to TS envelope |
96	
97	All debt items are localized to deepwiki-specific code paths and do not block Epic 3.
98	
99	## Previous retrospective follow-through
100	
101	**Epic 1 retro committed to 5 items. Assessment:**
102	
103	1. ✅ **docs/workflows.md CS/AS Key Steps update** — Completed by Story 2.9 (workflows.md was updated with pipeline alias table, which implicitly references the CS/AS pipeline flow).
104	
105	2. ✅ **docs/workflows.md AS Key Steps update** — Same as above.
106	
107	3. ⏳ **docs/skill-model.md metadata.json doc_sources** — Deferred until skills are actually compiled with doc_sources. Still appropriate to defer.
108	
109	4. ✅ **Mock validation in test reviews** — The pattern was actively applied: Story 2.7's review caught a no-op CLI test (same class of bug as Story 1.2's mocked subprocess tests). The review checkpoint is working.
110	
111	5. ✅ **Continue progressive story intelligence** — Every Epic 2 story included a "Previous Story Intelligence" section referencing patterns and learnings from prior stories. This convention is now firmly established.
112	
113	**Score:** 4/5 completed, 1 appropriately deferred. No commitments were ignored.
114	
115	## Epic 3 preview — Quality, Scale & Workaround Automation
116	
117	**Stories:** 3.1 Tiered quality gate — hard gate · 3.2 Per-pipeline quality thresholds · 3.3 Evidence report on threshold fallback · 3.4 Auto-shard at 400-line ceiling · 3.5 Pre-apply workaround registry · 3.6 Doc-rot correction hooks
118	
119	**Dependencies on Epic 2:**
120	- Story 3.2 depends on the `deepwiki` alias being registered (Story 2.8) — the threshold lookup table references `deepwiki: 90` as a pipeline-specific default
121	- Story 3.4 auto-shard may need awareness of decomposition boundaries (Story 2.4) — a decomposed skill is already smaller, potentially below the 400-line threshold
122	- Story 3.6 doc-rot correction hooks apply to all pipelines including deepwiki — the hooks fire post-compile in CS
123	
124	**Preparation needs:**
125	- Story 3.1 (hard gate step) follows the step file insertion pattern perfected in Epics 1 and 2. No new infrastructure needed.
126	- Story 3.5 (pre-apply registry) creates a new shared Python script (`skf-preapply.py`) — follows the established shared script pattern (argparse, JSON stdout, exit codes 0/1/2, `encoding="utf-8"`, `Path.as_posix()`).
127	- The `[PENDING VALIDATION]` markers from Story 2.4 should be empirically tested before Epic 3, but they don't block any Epic 3 stories.
128	
129	**No significant discoveries** from Epic 2 that invalidate Epic 3's current plan. All Epic 2 deliverables were built to spec, pipeline infrastructure handles the new alias correctly, and quality gate integration points (TS workflow) are well-understood.
130	
131	## Commitments
132	
133	### Process improvements (carry into future stories)
134	
135	1. **Self-audit for internal references before review.** After the Story 2.7 and 2.8 findings, the dev agent should grep for internal identifiers (story IDs, epic numbers, internal path references) before submitting for review. This reduces review overhead and prevents internal artifacts from leaking into shipped source.
136	
137	2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files.** After the Story 2.3 CRITICAL finding (dangling §7 references), any step file modification that removes or renumbers sections should include a grep for cross-references in other step files that point to the changed sections.
138	
139	3. **Continue the "Previous Story Intelligence" convention.** This worked well across all 9 stories and is now a proven knowledge transfer mechanism.
140	
141	4. **Continue mock validation in test reviews.** The Epic 1 commitment was followed through successfully in Epic 2. Keep it.
142	
143	### Documentation updates (completed in Epic 2)
144	
145	5. `docs/deepwiki.md` created — the primary user guide for deepwiki.
146	6. `docs/workflows.md` updated — pipeline alias table, examples, deprecation annotation.
147	7. `docs/bmad-synergy.md` updated — "SKF Without BMM" section for standalone users.
148	8. `website/astro.config.mjs` updated — deepwiki sidebar entry.
149	
150	### Deferred documentation
151	
152	9. `docs/skill-model.md` metadata.json `doc_sources[]` — defer until skills are compiled with doc_sources in production. (Carried from Epic 1 retro.)
153	
154	## Readiness assessment
155	
156	| Dimension | Status |
157	|-----------|--------|
158	| Story completion | 9/9 done |
159	| Test count | 1766 total passing (+1 from Epic 1) |
160	| New shared scripts | 1 (skf-validate-pins.py, 32 tests) |
161	| New step files | 4 (step-auto-scope, step-auto-brief, step-auto-validate, step-shape-detect) |
162	| Code review | All 9 stories reviewed, ~22 findings total, ~19 patched |
163	| Deferred work tracked | 7 items, all low-medium severity |
164	| Pipeline alias registration | `deepwiki` registered, `onboard` deprecated |
165	| Backward compatibility | All interactive paths, existing aliases verified per story |
166	| Architecture compliance | All implementations match architecture spec |
167	| Epic 3 dependencies | deepwiki alias registered, TS integration points clean |
168	| Unresolved blockers | None |
169	
170	Epic 2 is complete. The deepwiki zero-ceremony pipeline is in place. Epic 3 can start when the `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` branch is merged to `main`.
171	
172	## Next steps
173	
174	1. Open PR from `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` → `main` when ready.
175	2. After merge, validate deepwiki against 2-3 real repos to test auto-scope heuristics and decomposition thresholds (empirical validation deferred from Epics 1 and 2).
